companies.name: ZeroMark, Inc.
companies.description:
Problem
We dominate in the air and at sea — but on the ground, $500 drones outmaneuver warfighters still aiming by hand. In close combat, even elite shooters miss — and that’s where we lose the advantage.

Solution
ZeroMark adds real-time auto-aiming to rifles and weapon stations, physically steering the weapon like a gimbal so shooters stay on target — without changing how they operate or when they fire.

Field Validation
Live-fire validated at TREX 25, L-SHORAD, and Red Sands Hard Kill.

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 7 — integrated into fielded weapons and proven on rifles, crew-served platforms, and vehicle-mounted systems in live-fire conditions.

Strategic Advantage
ZeroMark requires no change to doctrine or behavior. Operators aim and fire as always. Our dataset enables real-time target segmentation — hitting motors, batteries, or electronics for maximum kill probability. No other system combines human-in-the-loop control with automated precision.

Go-to-Market Access
Engaged via AFWERX SBIR Phase III, JUON deployments, and OTs. Active with SOCOM, AFSOC, NSWC, and allied MODs. Integrating with primes on RWS and mobile C-UAS platforms.

Dual-Use Potential
Law enforcement, VIP security, infrastructure defense, and hunting safety — anywhere precise firearm use under pressure is critical.

Team
Founded by MongoDB’s former CISO. Team includes Tier 1 operators, ex-Meta and Google AI leads, and BD leaders from Anduril. Tactical depth meets technical execution.

Competitive Landscape
Smart Shooter gates the trigger. ZeroMark moves the weapon. We deliver physical auto-aiming with the shooter in control.

Primary User
Dismounted gunners & crew-served teams responsible for drone defeat and force protection.

User-Critical Problem
When drones appear, gunners have seconds to aim and shoot. Under stress, they miss. ZeroMark keeps them locked on target and alive.

companies.name: Nooks
companies.description:
Problem: Small businesses face a major barrier to defense work: prohibitive cost and complexity of securing accredited Sensitive Compartmented Information Facilities (SCIFs) and security clearances. This stifles innovation and limits the government's access to vital technology from non-traditional vendors.

Solution: subscriptions, shared SCIFs, expert services to get Facility Clearance (FCL) and Personnel Clearance (PCL).

Validation: Operational in Colorado Springs, supporting multiple classified projects. Expanding to Washington D.C. and Southern California+ 10 over next year.

Maturity: TRL 9 - operational, active customers.

Advantage: First-mover in creating scalable, multi-tenant SCIF network. Physical footprint + proprietary process for navigating clearances, = hard to copy.

G2M: enable SMBs to pursue opportunities through AFWERX, SBIR, DIU, and primes' partnerships by removing capital and time cost of building SCIFs.

Dual-Use: model extends to commercial sectors with high-security needs, including finance, regulated R&D, and critical infrastructure, where secure physical space is needed.

Team: Founded by defense operators and GovCon pros with deep national security expertise and agile startup experience.

Competitors: traditional "build-your-own" SCIF model & large prime contractors. We are a shared-economy model that dramatically lowers barrier to entry.

User: A founder or lead engineer at a small defense technology startup.

User's Critical Problem: The inability to discuss classified requirements or access secure networks, which prevents them from closing high-value deals and collaborating with government partners.

companies.name: Pulse Space
companies.description:
Problem
In future conflicts, dominance will be determined by control of the battlespace above national borders—including air and orbital domains. A nation that cannot secure and command the space above its territory surrenders a critical element of its sovereignty and strategic defense. It no longer controls it's fate. 

Solution
We are building the ability to project force at vast distances instantaneously using high-energy lasers in space. 

Field Validation
Pre-Field

Technology Maturity (TRL)
We are currently at TRL 5. We have fully built a flatsat in the lab. Tested in various simulated environments. 

Strategic Advantage
This is hard to copy for a few reasons. We have IP and are quickly growing our portfolio. We also have unique 

Go-to-Market Access
SBIRs, sponsored for a STRATFI, and we have commercial contracts already. 

Dual-Use Potential
Yes. Our commercial focuses are remote power, space situational awareness, and communications in orbit. 

Team
Karl Stedman - I build things. Self-taught. Been through hell to get here. 
Stuart Volkow - DOD Analyst turned CubeSat and high-altitude balloon project manager. 
John Stanford - Was the CTO of Ubiquiti Networks from 100k to over a 1B in sales and through the IPO. 

Competitive Landscape
Officially, no one. Unofficially - I expect competition from Anduril, Raytheon, and a couple of startups. 

Primary User
Space Force - Delta 9, Delta 3
Golden Dome operators. TBD

User-Critical Problem
U.S. satellites are highly exposed in today’s contested space environment. While our forces depend on space-based assets for communications, navigation, targeting, and ISR, most satellites were not built for wartime conditions. They are predictable, undefended, and easy targets.

companies.name: Gallatin AI
companies.description:
Problem
Today's logisticians suffer from fragmented logistics common operating pictures, inaccurate demand forecasting, and highly manual sustainment planning, hobbling our ability to make decisions at the speed of relevance to large scale combat.

Solution
Navigator is an AI decision platform that includes an intuitive logistics common operating picture, a demand forecasting module and a resupply course of action generation engine.

Field Validation
Fielding across Army units in 2025.

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 6 forecasting achieving TRL 7 by the end of 2025 after several field training exercises.

Strategic Advantage
Our technology is designed to continuously improve with use, creating network effects. Our team includes several experienced defense tech leaders. We maintain a network of retired military logisticians who inform every aspect of development.

Go-to-Market Access
Currently have a SBIR and corporate partnerships with several primes

Dual-Use Potential
We made our first commercial sale for logistics decision support in emergency response.

Team
Co-Founder/CEO - Formerly Scale AI federal  leader and Palantir engineer.
Co-Founder/CTO - Pioneer of drone delivery, including standing up Amazon Prime Air.
Co-Founder/CPO - Founded a dual-use logistics tech company that exited via acquisition

Competitive Landscape
Within the Army, many units have seen many solutions and assess Gallatin as the strongest.

Primary User
Logistics cells of tactical through theater HQs (S4, G4, A4, J4) and planners within logistics units (AF LRS', Army TSC's, etc.). We have two distinct product areas, segmented  by tactical logistics and theater logistics.

User-Critical Problem
Today’s logistics workflows will not scale to meet the demands of near-peer conflict.

companies.name: Skyline Nav AI, Inc.
companies.technologyArea: Trusted AI / Autonomy,Advanced Computing and Software
companies.description:
Problem: Loss of GPS or signal makes precise, real-time positioning for vehicles or devices impossible in mission-critical operations.

Solution: Pathfinder delivers meter-level absolute position using computer vision and precomputed satellite data, no GPS needed. In areas with no discernible terrain features, Pathfinder leverages visual odometry and IMU for temporary relative localization until Pathfinder can recognize discernible terrain features. 

Field Validation: Tested with the US Air Force, NASA, Army, BAE Systems, Kearfott, Isuzu; tested in 40+ global regions and live commercial/military projects.

Technology Maturity (TRL): TRL 7; multiple field trials, real-world deployments with often centimeter accuracy and commercial programs.

Strategic Advantage: Proprietary algorithms, global dataset integration, sensor-agnostic fusion, extensive field testing, and rapid deployment pipeline.

Go-to-Market Access: Secured contracts via AFWERX, NASA, direct sales to primes (BAE Systems, Kearfott), and commercial customers (e.g. Isuzu).

Dual-Use Potential: Used in defense, logistics, fleet, disaster response, autonomous mobility, aerospace, and AI safety across industries.

Team: Founder: Kanwar Singh (CEO & Army Captain), Dr. Rajeev Surati (R&D, ex-MIT), team from Harvard, Microsoft, Fidelity & other organizations.

Competitive Landscape: Incumbents across magnetic, IMU and quantum (GPS/Alt-PNT); Pathfinder offers real-time, sensor-agnostic and multi-modal absolute and relative localization by leveraging leverages cameras and IMUs. 

Primary User: Pilots, drone operators, fleet leads, JTACs, vehicle operators, and logistics coordinators.

User-Critical Problem: Operators cannot trust GPS in denied/jammed settings, leading to mission failures, and life/death situations.

companies.name: Cachai
companies.technologyArea: Advanced Computing and Software,Trusted AI / Autonomy
companies.description:
Problem
Today’s “swarming” autonomy fails under pressure. This endangers warfighters and flummoxes integration architects:
-- One node acts as leader or hub—disable it, and coordination collapses.  
-- No shared state: each drone has a different view of the mission
-- No consensus: tasks are duplicated, dropped, or never completed
-- Timing is inconsistent: one node thinks it’s 14:00, another 14:03
-- Integrators are stuck writing fragile glue code to hold everything together
-- New nodes require manual trust/config — no dynamic mesh, no resilience
-- Updates risk “bricking” nodes without orchestration or rollback

The result: warfighters babysit machines, and autonomy can't scale.

Solution
Altiro by Cachai is a distributed autonomy engine that enables real-time state sharing, consensus, and coordination across heterogeneous platforms—with no cloud, no central brain, and no single point of failure.

Validation
At the July 2025 OUSD(R&E) T-REX event, we maintained a live Kubernetes cluster across multiple Raspberry Pis over wireless. These nodes shared mission state as a stand-in for live autonomous vehicles. August demos add vehicle integration. Logs and video available on request.

Anticipated traction:
$600K subcontract from Leidos (DEVCOM, late 2025)
$100K NGREA-funded deployment via INDOPACOM units
$1M follow-on OTA in pipeline (T-REX, AFWERX, xTech, SOCOM)
Charging $30K for commercial pilots; 10+ expected in next 12 months

Strategic Edge
Most distributed systems experts are trained on hyperscale cloud. We built Altiro from first principles for the tactical edge. Patent pending. Two-year head start. No one else is this far along.

User
Altiro is "used" by GS-12–14 autonomy engineers at primes, not the warfighter directly. These integrators want to stop duct-taping systems together—and finally have infrastructure built for contested autonomy.

companies.name: Defense Unicorns
companies.warfareDomain: Air, Land, Sea, Space, Cyber, Information, Multi
companies.description:
Problem
Delivering software to the edge and updating it on demand while meeting ATO requirements is nearly impossible

Solution
UDS integrates apps into a secure/compliant platform. UDS Tactical Edge brings that platform to edge systems and makes it easy for non-technical warfighters to make software updates.

Field Validation
UDS is in use in many DoD systems, including AF Defensive Cyber and Navy Project Blue. UDS Tactical Edge has been proven on multiple fighter platforms and C-17s.

TRL
UDS is TRL 8 - Fully integrated in a pre-production government system
UDS Tactical Edge is TRL 7 - Prototype demonstrated in operational environment

Strategic Advantage
While core parts of UDS are open source to avoid vendor lock, our ability to use and implement UDS is the best in the world. We also employ maintainers of open-source projects that are crucial to DoD operations, such as Kubernetes.

Go-to-Market Access
GSA, SBIR, Tradewinds, Platform One Marketplace, and direct awards

Dual-Use Potential
Any regulated market can benefit from UDS

Team
Multiple from Air Force software factories, including Rob Slaughter and Jeff McCoy
Maintainers for key open source products, including Kubernetes

Competitive Landscape
Others provide platforms to accelerate ATOs. No one else extends their platforms to edge environments.

Primary User
For edge systems, maintainers and operators can use UDS Tactical Edge mobile app to deploy software. Weapon system SPOs will maintain the authorized repo of software for deployment.

User-Critical Problem
Software is the core of every weapon system and updating it quickly is key to our strategic advantage. Today updates take up to a year and millions of dollars. We make that cheap, fast, and repeatable without requiring technical expertise in the field.

companies.name: ARES Security
companies.description:
Problem: Security Forces, operating below authorized staffing levels, do not have the personnel or systems to defend against evolving threats. 

Solution: ARES Security provides autonomous systems, C2, and AI/Analytics to secure expeditionary and fixed sites against evolving threats.

Field Validation: DTRA, 435th CRG, Tyndall, Dyess, GVSC, 70% of US commercial nuclear plants, the World Trade Center, and many more.  

Technology Maturity (TRL): TRL9. Evidence: DTRA program of record, independent DoD VV&A, 435th deployment, commercial accounts. 

Strategic Advantage: DTRA uses our tech to protect our special weapons. Our C2 engine provides security for the World Trade Center with over 150K sensors and 8 different video management systems. Today 1 operator plans missions and operates multiple autonomous platforms. We have invested over $120M in this tech and protect it with multiple patents. 

Go-to-Market Access: 15 Phase I / II, TACFI, Tyndall CSO, Dyess Phase III, DTRA Program of Record. 

Dual-Use Potential: ARES provides physical security solutions in 70% of the US Commercial Nuclear market, the World Trade Center and many more.  

Team: The ARES executive team has worked together for over 25 years. This team developed and delivered the 911 CAD system to NYPD, the largest 911 account in the world. This was an early testament to the technical excellence, functional superiority, and political savvy of the ARES team.

Competitive Landscape: Competitors focus on niche capabilities. ARES provides an integrated capability for Security Forces with VV&A and deployment credentials. 

User-Critical Problem: In TSgt Morris, 435th words: “The force multiplying ability that AVERT provides to the CRG mission is unmatched and this exercise proved just that over a 5-day period.”

Team: The ARES executive team has worked together for over 25 years. This team developed and delivered the 911 CAD system to NYPD, the largest 911 account in the world. This was an early testament to the technical excellence, functional superiority, and political savvy of the ARES team.

Competitive Landscape: Competitors focus on niche capabilities. ARES provides an integrated capability for Security Forces with VV&A and deployment credentials. 

User-Critical Problem: In TSgt Morris, 435th words: “The force multiplying ability that AVERT provides to the CRG mission is unmatched and this exercise proved just that over a 5-day period.”

companies.name: Duality Systems
companies.technologyArea: Advanced Computing and Software,Trusted AI / Autonomy,Human-Machine Interfaces
companies.description:
Problem
Warfighters and mission planners face friction aligning personnel and resources to mission needs. Across flight, cyber, and logistics units, manual scheduling degrades readiness, misallocates talent, burns out teams, and introduces operational risk.

Solution
Balance Scheduler is an AI platform that delivers optimized personnel and resource schedules in minutes—up to 90% faster than legacy tools. It automates workflows, adapts to mission constraints, and accounts for certifications and training, freeing skilled talent for higher-value work.

Field Validation
Used in U.S. civilian healthcare and SPACECOM/JCO; now adapting (SBIR Phase II) for Space Force. Prior SBIRs supported UAV and missileer planning.

TRL
TRL 7 — Commercially deployed with active defense prototyping.

Strategic Advantage
Proprietary optimization engine for constraint-heavy ops. API-first, integration-ready, and adaptable—no data lock-in.

Go-to-Market
Via past SBIRS (1-3), direct contracting, OTA, consortia (DIBC, NSTXL, SpEC), and partnerships.

Dual-Use
Relevant in healthcare, logistics, energy, and more—anywhere resource agility matters.

Team
Former USAF officers and technology ops experts with deep experience (gov and commercial) in AI, scheduling, and defense tech delivery. Includes PhD engineers, MIT-trained data scientists, and former leaders in federal health and cyber innovation.

Competitive Landscape
PEX, Excel, and Puckboard lack adaptability. Duality offers scalable, flexible AI optimization built for defense operations.

Primary User
Front-line schedulers and ops planners—plus CCs, readiness shops, and others needing visibility into personnel and asset availability.

User-Critical Problem
“My LIFE is scheduling now. Every change costs time, morale, and mission readiness.”

companies.name: X-Hab 3D, Inc.
companies.description:
Problem Skilled labor is down 40%, material costs up, logistics strained. This is a national security threat. Building mission-critical infrastructure for ACE and dispersed basing is too slow, manpower-heavy, and supply-chain dependent. Tents and manual labor delay ops, expose troops.

Solution Automate construction. X-Hab 3D’s mobile, robotic 3D concrete printing (3DCP) system prints vertical structures (barracks, barriers) and pours flatwork (runway repair) using local materials. Operates with 2–3 people, off-grid, all-terrain, and ships assembled in a 20′ container.

Field Validation Tested by MDANG, homebuilders in KY and AK.

TRL 7/8

Strategic Advantage Only dual-use, DoD-compliant mobile 3DCP system. Defense drives the tech—commercial markets scale it.

Go-to-Market DARPA, NASA, HUD, USAF contracts. 2025 sales to Tyndall AFB, Penn State ARL, HSH Drywall. MDANG APFIT proposal (down-selected) requests 15 units. Direct sales; beta users now marketers.

Dual-Use Potential $150B+ global market; $30B U.S. SAM: 20,000 U.S. firms; SOM: 1,000. Focus: housing, commercial, disaster recovery.

Team CEO Bruce Kraselsky has 7 prior ventures. Co-founders Bilén (engineering) and Duarte (architecture), Penn State professors and AddCon Lab founders, led Penn State to NASA Mars Habitat finals. Dr. Quercia is a global expert in concrete composites. Penn State is an investor; ARL a key collaborator.

Competition RIC, Cybe, Constructions 3D, ICE focus on commercial. Only X-Hab 3D offers dual-use system built for military.

Primary Users Red Horse, Seabees, National Guard, USACE, Army/Marine engineers, Prime BEEF, others.

User Problem Resilient infrastructure is hard to build with limited crews, rough terrain, and heavy logistics. Warfighters need a faster, expeditionary solution.

companies.name: ElectricFish Energy Inc
companies.productName: ANCHOR Microgrid for Operational Energy Resilience
companies.technologyArea: Energy Resilience,Trusted AI / Autonomy,Integrated Sensing and Cyber
companies.description:
Modern bases and forward sites are caught between an aging grid— 70 % of U.S. equipment is past service life—and high-peak loads that AI clusters, C5ISR, eVTOL pads, and fleet chargers will triple by 2028. Grid upgrades cost ≈ $1.3 M / MW and take years, while the stop-gap “genset + UPS + battery trailer” is loud, fuel-hungry, and risky: JP-8 convoys run $100–$400 / gal and invite casualties.
ElectricFish solves this with ANCHOR, a C-130-deployable microgrid that unifies a 400 kWh battery, 350 kW bidirectional charger, and patented Stargazer™ AI to deliver 99.99 % power quality in < 20 ms on a 30 kW feeder or JP-8 genset, then monetize idle kWh through grid services.
The core 350² hardware is field-proven—six commercial units have been deployed since 2022, twenty-five more shipping next year—placing ANCHOR at TRL 8. Four issued U.S. patents (11,769,094; 11,681,967; 11,270,243; 11,007,891) cover our ML-driven micro-grid optimization and extreme-fast-charge resource adequacy, creating a strong moat, while a sealed, liquid-cooled design and physics-informed degradation control ensure reliable, all-climate operation from -40 °C to +55 °C. Go-to-market begins with our fleet-charging sales channel, then spins off variants—ANCHOR for expeditionary energy, XDC for edge data centers—into DoD via AFWERX TACFI, DIU CSOs, PM E2S2 OTAs, NavalX Tech Bridges, and NSIC hardware acceleration, scaling to IDIQs with primes already serving Navy and Army bases. Dual-use opportunities span commercial fleets, utility VPPs, disaster relief, and colocation GPUs, tapping a $10 B+ market.

companies.name: Zephr.xyz Inc.
companies.technologyArea: Advanced Computing and Software,Integrated Sensing and Cyber
companies.description:
Problem
Jamming and spoofing attacks challenges PNT across the globe. T-ROK systems are expensive, large profile (targets) that need a lot of bandwidth to network. 

Solution
Zephr has built a capability to use smartphones (TAK devices) to create a sensor network to map areas of jamming/spoofing impact and geolocate emitters for targeting. It can run locally or networked.

Field Validation
The Zephr system has been extensively tested against jamming in Ukraine as well as teleportation/push spoofers in Israel. The team will be testing at APEX, Jammertest and UK NAVWAR.

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 7
 
Strategic Advantage
Zephr uses the cheap omni-directional smartphone antennas to determine directionality + magnitude of jamming/spoofing attacks. Our multi-receiver networking capability fuses data into a COP. Patents 12,038,513, 12,061,268, 12,117,533

Go-to-Market Access
The team has a D2P2 SBIR with AFRL to integrate with TAK. We've also worked with the NSIN Propel program, attending exercises above and partnered with TAG the MGUE prime. 

Dual-Use Potential
Zephr's commercial positioning system and AI navigation for smartphones is being piloted by Honda, Suzuki, Meta, Grab, Uber, Overture, Ericsson, Rx Networks, Amazon and Samsung

Team
The Zephr team has built three previous startup in the geospatial domain with exits to ESRI, Maxar and Snapchat. The team is over 50% PhD's. 

Competitive Landscape
The legacy T-ROK systems are sold by Thales/BAE - Zephr is cheaper, low profile, low weight, low bandwidth and integrates with TAK.

Primary User
TAK users in contested environments - specifically CEWOs in SOCOM, Army, AFSOC

User-Critical Problem
Your TAK device is being jammed or spoofed and you want to either target the emitter or maneuver around the impacted area.

companies.name: FibrX
companies.warfareDomain: Sea, Land, Air, Information, Space, Cyber, Multi
companies.technologyArea: Integrated Sensing and Cyber,Trusted AI / Autonomy,Maritime
companies.description:
Problem
Critical assets across the military degrade silently from corrosion, fatigue, and thermal stress—causing unplanned failures, safety risks, and billions in maintenance costs due to a lack of real-time structural visibility.

Solution
FibrX provides real-time structural health monitoring using fiber optics and AI to detect corrosion, strain, and thermal anomalies before failure occurs.

Field Validation
Pre-field

Technology Maturity (TRL)
Currently at TRL 4 — validated in a lab environment with a full system prototype, and integration into relevant platforms scheduled for Q1 2026 field testing.

Strategic Advantage
Partially exclusive U.S. Navy license

Go-to-Market Access
Engaged through SBIR (NASA, AFWERX), FedTech, NAVSEA, and direct with defense primes and agencies via CRADA and pilot programs.

Dual-Use Potential
High-impact in energy (offshore wind, nuclear), aerospace (MRO), semiconductors (cleanroom monitoring), EVs, and infrastructure (bridges, water systems). Total addressable market exceeds $3.9 trillion, including $2.5T in global corrosion costs alone (NACE).

Team
FibrX is led by Kamel Greene, a former DOE national security contractor and deep tech founder, alongside Michael Cupples, a 30-year Raytheon veteran in 6DOF simulations and aerospace systems. The team includes talent from NASA, SAIC, MPOs, and top academic labs, with deep expertise in sensing, AI, and government infrastructure.

Competitive Landscape
Luna Innovations offers expensive, bulky systems; startups focus on niche sensor types. FibrX delivers a lightweight, low-SWaP, cloud-ready solution with multi-domain deployment flexibility.

Primary User
Fleet maintenance leads, aerospace MRO teams, and sustainment commanders overseeing high-value, high-risk platforms.

User-Critical Problem
They can’t see structural failures coming—leading to mission delays, asset loss, and safety risks.

companies.name: Spectrum Defense Systems
companies.description:
Problem:
Modern EW training and deception missions lack affordable, modular systems that emit realistic RF signals across wide frequency ranges. Existing solutions are costly, inflexible, and hard to operate.

Solution:
The Dynamic Radio Emulator System (DRES) is a low-cost, modular RF emitter covering 20 MHz–1 GHz and 20 MHz–6 GHz. Swappable amplifier modules let users tailor power and frequency output to mission needs. The system is remotely controlled via ATAK.

Field Validation & TRL:
TRL 7 — Demonstrated in operational environments with 3rd Infantry Division at NTC, JRTC, JMRC, and with the Florida National Guard. Used at VANGUARD 2024 to simulate radar threats.

Strategic Advantage:
We are patenting our system architecture, ATAK integration, and custom electronics. Combined with field-proven workflows and strong unit relationships, this creates both technical and relational barriers to entry.

Go-to-Market Access:
Our pricing is below key procurement thresholds, enabling units to buy using discretionary funds. This led to a $150k purchase by 3ID and $55k by FLNG, with multiple additional units preparing to procure. This bottom-up traction supports our path toward PEO EW&C.

Dual-Use Potential:
Our long-range transceiver (TXVR) enables disaster response, off-grid comms, and remote industrial ops where infrastructure is limited.

Team:
Co-Founders Joseph Petersen (CEO, Purdue ECE) and Alina Miles (CFO, ONU MechE) lead system development. 

Competitive Landscape:
DRES is more affordable, ATAK-integrated, and scalable than any alternative—supporting 20–40+ nodes in a mesh network. No other system offers this combination.

Primary User:
CEMA operators from platoon NCOs to brigade-level leaders.

User-Critical Problem:
Current systems delay training, require contractors, and limit control. Operators need scalable, fieldable emitters can be managed independently.

companies.name: Swarmbotics AI
companies.technologyArea: Trusted AI / Autonomy,Human-Machine Interfaces,Integrated Sensing and Cyber
companies.description:
Problem
Current robotics program are too expensive, too fragile, and the form factor isn’t conducive to human-machine integration.

Solution
Our solution combines an AI software C2 layer with small, attritable UGVs to carry modular payloads in heterogenous swarms.

Field Validation
Project Convergence, SOCOM TE, Northern Strike, Thunder Strike, SMEx, Uncrewed Triple Challenge, Army xTechOverwatch, and others

Technology Maturity (TRL)
5-6, self-evaluated at demos in relevant operational conditions

Strategic Advantage
Creating immediate and safe human-machine integration to experiment payloads with maneuver units, informing DOTMLPF / TTP formation around robotics. Urgent use cases include C-UAS, EW, ISR, MILDEC, and kinetic terrain shaping.

Go-to-Market Access
We have funding through innovation arms and certain program offices, and are also selling to defense primes.

Dual-Use Potential
PHYSEC

Team
Stephen Houghton, CEO; former COO of Embark Trucks, VP at GM Cruise, VP Amazon Zoox, McKinsey, Harvard, Stanford, USMC
Drew Watson, CTO; former product ops Embark, NASA JPL, CIA robotics

Competitive Landscape
Most competitors have larger form factors. Other small UGV makers have constrained use cases. 

Primary User
Maneuver ground units (FORSCOM, SOCOM)

User-Critical Problem
Humans are needlessly exposed to danger / risk best assumed by robots; they are mission-constrained with strained sensor-to-shooter loop.

companies.name: Radius Method
companies.description: Mission software teams struggle with speed. While AI offers acceleration, DoD programs lack secure, compliant deployment for autonomous workflows, especially in IL5+ and disconnected environments. The issue isn't model access, but operationalizing them securely and repeatedly across the full software lifecycle. Crystal Tower solves this. It's a secure, AI-native software factory enabling governed, provable, and deployable agent workflows that boost developer output 10–20x, transforming DevSecOps into secure cognition. Already fielded in 5GAT, PEO-STRI UnITE, and Kessel Run, Crystal Tower supports govt and vendor teams delivering critical capabilities today. With a TRL-9, it's deployed in live environments, driving outcomes, meeting compliance and Zero Trust. Crystal Tower's foundation—a ModSecOps framework for agent governance, prompt control, and cognitive auditability, integrated into hardened IL4+ pipelines—makes it hard to replicate. This, plus early traction through SBIR, AFWERX, and leading primes, gives Radius Method a durable lead. The platform extends to energy, AI safety, and autonomous logistics, where secure, accountable automation is crucial. Radius Method’s team brings firsthand commercial and government expertise from Platform One, Kessel Run, TRMC, and PEO STRI, having deployed platforms where others failed. While others focus on tools or model access, Crystal Tower delivers operational agent workflows with built-in governance, fielded today. Our users are software leads, test teams, and DevOps engineers in classified and tactical programs. Their reality: brittle pipelines, long compliance delays, and inability to integrate AI. Crystal Tower replaces this with secure, compliant, agent-enabled workflows, accelerating delivery without compromising control.

companies.name: DataSoft Corp.
companies.description:
Problem

Team members without a radio so they have to use voice and hand signals to communicate. 
Solution

The RAP (Radio Access Point) is a low-SWAP module that attaches to the RED side of tactical radio (117, 163, 167, 148, 152 etc.) and extends radio network 1300 feet in range so that operators can connect via their smartphone EUDs/ATAK. The MTR (Miniature Tactical Router) is a low-SWAP module that attaches on the BLK side of a tactical radio (117, 163, 167 etc) and provides a VPN connection to SATCOM (Iridium, BGAN, StarLink) terminal or LTE/5G cellular device for BLOS comms.
Field Validation

RAP has been extensively tested/evaluated by USMC and NIWC. The MTR for 117G radio is deployed by Netherlands and German SoF
Technology Maturity (TRL)
[State your current TRL. Cite evidence: lab demo, prototyping, field trials, live deployment.]
RAP is at TRL 8, MTR-117 is at TRL 9 and MTR-MM is TRL 7
Strategic Advantage

RAP is NSA certified for Tactical CSfC 
Go-to-Market Access

Responding to BAAs CSOs, and RFIs, collaborating with prime contractors and trusted integrators. RAP and MTR are on the NASA SEWP IDIQ and Army's CHS-6 catalog
Dual-Use Potential

None
Team

Our team has electrical, mechanical, software engineers with over 25 years of experience C4 system design and development.
Competitive Landscape

The RAP is the only low-SWAP product on the NSA's CSfC Components List for Tactical Capability solutions 
Primary User

Dismount soldier/Marine/airman, TACP operator, SoF operator, coalition partner

[Translate strategic pain into the user's daily operational friction.]

companies.name: BlackBox Energy Systems
companies.description:
Problem
Motors that are inefficient, hot, and loud--and not made in America

Solution
The TruVolt® is a rugged, compact, axial flux motor built for high performance and low heat signature, with near-silent operation and integrated electronics all in one ruggedized 2hp, 12lb unit. 

Field Validation
We’ve delivered a custom motor for an underwater drone, and they want more.

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 7-8 live deployment.

Strategic Advantage
The stator design is patented, the coil winding apparatus is proprietary and non-obvious, and the electronics and firmware are proprietary.

Go-to-Market Access
Navy SBIR, and direct contacts to OEMs, seeking additional SBIRs and connections to primes.

Dual-Use Potential
Excellent and competitively priced option for fans, blowers, conveyors, pumps, compressors, and is capable of servoing. The integrated controller features VFD functionality, performance sensors, and is PLC programmable, enabling the replacement of multiple motor configurations with one motor. We have LOIs for 10,000+ units once we can deliver.

Team
Dr. Elijah Jensen (PhD physics, and Master's EE), PI on multiple NASA STTRs, 12 years of space-based power systems with work on the ISS and Artemis mission. Joshua Rimmer, COO, 15 years Army, Bronze Star, and 16 years manufacturing.

Competitive Landscape
Axial Flux competitors--ECM, great small PCB motor, lower horsepower. Evolito by YASA weighs 13kg--we are 12lb. We are filling an in-between size for axial flux motor technology.

Primary User
Unmanned and autonomous systems OEMs

User-Critical Problem
Choice of American-made motors with the power density, quiet, low-thermal signature, and efficient performance of the TruVolt in the 1/2hp-7.5hp range needed for group 1 and 2 UAVs are very limited.

companies.name: DigitalCM LLC
companies.warfareDomain: Air, Sea, Space, Cyber, Land, Multi
companies.description:
Our Immersive Training Network (ITN) addresses the core challenges of military training, limited time, constrained funding, different standards and lack of consistency, by delivering standardized, on-demand access to immersive learning. Leveraging AR/VR, an AI-driven LMS, and digital twin technology, ITN enables full-scenario training, mission planning, and COA development accessible anytime, anywhere, on any device. Learning options include interactive manuals, equipment-specific training, maintenance support, and fully immersive instructor-led experiences.

We have invested over $5M and thousands of development hours into building this advanced ecosystem. Actively used in our Tampa, Florida training programs, ITN has driven measurable improvements in student retention and course completion rates while supporting both hands-on exercises and enterprise-level training initiatives.

Building on this proven success, we are expanding beyond defense into the commercial sector, positioning ITN as the premier immersive training platform for both DoD and industry. Some of our applications are live on the App store, with additional releases coming soon.

Our Founder and CEO, a former Special Forces/SMU operator, brings extensive experience in training, technical integration, red teaming, and SOF-focused technology development. He has instructed over 250 multi-domain classes, established five programs of record, and developed sensitive capabilities for the SOF community, including authoring multiple white papers on advanced technologies.

ITN bridges technology and training, creating a future where readiness is achieved through innovation, scalability, and true mission-focused learning.

Spatial computing meets next-gen training to drive readiness anytime, anywhere!

companies.name: Divergent Space Technologies
companies.description:
**The Tactical Intelligence Gap**

Military intelligence analysts at the tactical edge can't access national overhead imagery—a critical operational blindspot that costs lives. While strategic commanders leverage exquisite sensors, frontline units operate blind.

We automate access to commercial ISR products, enabling tip/cue and multi-sensor fusion that compresses the sensor-to-shooter timeline from hours to minutes. Currently prototyping in containerized AWS infrastructure mirroring government cloud systems, we're partnering with USMC Space Intelligence for field exercises.

Our integration depth creates an uncopyable advantage. We democratize commercial imaging to match national system capabilities, opening dual-use markets in energy and logistics. Through Accenture Federal Services, we're positioned for major prime contracts.

The team brings unmatched credentials: Philip Brooks (13 years NRO, Mission Integration Directorate through 2024), Lynn Wright (former Director, Office of Naval Intelligence), and Frank Rose (former Deputy Director, National Nuclear Security Agency).

No direct competitors exist in this space. We serve a specific, underserved community: tactical intelligence personnel and signature management units historically denied ISR access. For operators needing real-time imagery to survive and succeed, we're the only solution bridging the gap between strategic assets and tactical needs.

companies.name: Safire Technology Group
companies.productName: Safire Products & Systems with Safer and High Density Batteries
companies.description:
Problem
Warfighters are forced to rely on underperforming batteries and fragmented energy infrastructure. This results in logistics complexity and increased exposure to threats from both the enemy and the elements.

Solution
Stratos Battery Pack: a modular, ruggedized, and safety-enhanced energy solution designed for seamless platform and system integration. Safire platforms include mobile energy storage trailers, renewable microgrid energy storage, electric dirt bikes, and extreme cold weather unmanned robotics. Stratos packs collect real-time battery data—feed it into SafireEOS, our advanced energy operating system. This data layer empowers real-time decision making and AI-driven logistics modeling to prevent failures and enhance operational readiness.

Field Validation
Stratos battery pack and electric dirt bike tested by 3 ASOS in field environment. Mobile and stationary storage prototypes at pre-field levels.

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 7 for key products through user field testing.

Strategic Advantage
Battery cell and pack safety IP, power electronics IP with exclusive data acess. 

Go-to-Market Access
AFWERX SBIRs, TACFI, and OTA contract.

Dual-Use Potential
Energy, energy management, and electric vehicles / UxVs.

Team
Safire is a team of experts in materials science, battery engineering, and systems integration from organizations such as Palantir, Tesla, and SOCOM.

Competitive Landscape
Other companies solving this problem use outdated battery chemistries, limited safety features, and lack electronics intelligence

Primary User
JTACs, CCTs, tactical operators, force protection, maintenance, expeditionary airfield establishment.

User-Critical Problem
More widespread and modular access to higher performing energy systems enables Agile Combat Employment.

companies.name: Kinnami
companies.productName: AmiShare - Secure Resilient Edge Data Mesh
companies.warfareDomain: Air, Sea, Land, Space, Cyber, Information, Multi
companies.technologyArea: Advanced Computing and Software,Trusted AI / Autonomy
companies.description:
Problem: Defense teams fail when they can’t access, share, or protect mission data in contested environments. Cloud systems break down, leaving edge users without intel to act or decide.

Solution: AmiShare is a secure data mesh software platform that lets distributed users—drones, vehicles, mobile teams—store, prioritize, and sync mission-critical data without persistent connectivity. It enforces strict access control and ensures data integrity across domains.

Field Validation: Validated with USAF (SBIR, TACFI), USACE, and NATO DIANA. Demonstrated on UAVs, mobile platforms, and disconnected networks. Pilots underway with British Telecom.

Technology Maturity (TRL): TRL 6: Operational prototypes tested in field; resilience validated in disconnected ops; progressing through Phase III/TACFI.

Strategic Advantage: AI-based policy control; no central infrastructure; hardware/network agnostic; zero-trust aligned; supports multi-path routing. Protected IP and deep edge integration make it hard to replicate.

Go-to-Market Access: AFWERX SBIR Phases I–III, TACFI, NATO DIANA, Congressional OTA funding, and direct pathways via primes and integrators.

Dual-Use Potential: Applies to energy (e.g., distributed solar), infrastructure, disaster response, autonomous logistics, and AI assurance.

Team: Founded by MIT, Veritas, and Symantec alumni. Team includes ex-Google and AWS engineers, enterprise technologists, and GTM experts.

Competitive Landscape: Unlike cloud-dependent platforms, AmiShare is purpose-built for secure, resilient data ops in disconnected, low-SWAP environments.

Primary User: Tactical operators, intel analysts, and platform integrators.

User-Critical Problem: When cloud fails, users need secure, local data—across devices—even when jammed or denied.

companies.name: Corvus Industries
companies.description:
Problem
sUAS are the deadliest weapon on the modern battlefield and increasingly impervious to EW and other non-kinetic defeat methods. 

Solution
A low cost, mini-CUAS AI Rocket Interceptor designed to kinetically defeat Group 1 & 2 sUAS. Interceptor leverages COTS components and on board edge AI for autonomous targeting & engagement without relying on GPS or RF emissions with a modular payload capability.

Field Validation
Pre-Field

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 5, prototyping and 10 non-field flight tests with core technologies demonstrated.

Strategic Advantage
Timing edge as well as team experience in developing COTS based missile enabling ultra low cost, currently integrating with unmanned platforms.

Go-to-Market Access
Applied for Direct to Phase II Open Topic SBIR with letter of support from AFWERX. Working with DIU OnRamp Hub and in discussion with Army DEVCOM.

Dual-Use Potential
Can have a modular payload including a low collateral defeat payload to protect critical civilian infrastructure.

Team
Co-Founder, EE, 9 years defense experience, CUAS/CIED/EW/Hypersonic/Missile dev & test. Co-founder, Prior venture backed startup founder/CEO $92m raised, $100m revenue, multiple patents. Engineers formerly from RTX, Northrup Grumman, Hyper Giant, Diligent Robotics.

Competitive Landscape
Startups and incumbents are focused on short range (gun based) kinetic defeat solutions as well as expensive, longer range interceptors. We target sUAS in the range between guns and existing, expensive, longer range systems with an ultra low cost solution.

Primary User
Infantry, security forces, and any other combat role that could be vulnerable to sUAS attacks.

User-Critical Problem
Outside of extremely short range engagements, there is no reliable way for individual infantry units to kinetically defeat individual or swarms of sUAS.

companies.name: Alitheon, Inc.
companies.description:
Supply Chain Integrity is at risk due to counterfeit parts and lack of reliable tracing. E.g. a million F-35 parts lost and fake computer boards installed in F-18s, B-52s. Why? DoD relies on easily compromised proxy barcodes, data plates, and tags.

FeaturePrint is software that serializes items just by taking a picture from a COTS camera, even a phone. What fingerprints are for people FeaturePrints are for things. 

It is deployed with commercial customers across sectors. In DoD achieved 100% accuracy in tests incl. 2 USAF and 1 Army Phase II for inventory management and anticounterfeiting.

TRL
In commercial production. DoD TRL 7 - testing use cases and need ATO.

Strategic Advantage
No proxies; COTS cameras and phones; 59 patents. Zero trust for physical items.

Go-to-Market Access
SBIR Phase I and II; Tradwinds

Dual-Use - Currently deployed with luxury goods, collectibles, art, healthcare, automotive and aviation, and precious metals.

Team - 20 experienced members incl. 
Roei Ganzarski, CEO, 30 years of business, software, and aerospace;
Mark Tocci, 30 years of software, Multithreading, Big Data, Algorithms, Pattern Recognition
Dr. Dave Stevens, 30 years of advanced mathematics, ML, and speech recognition.

Competitive Landscape
Only other company is German-based Bosch. Alitheon is only one with issued patents.

Primary User
Quartermasters, inventory leads, supply chain leads, service and maintenance leads.

User-Critical Problem
- Supply chain - can get duped by fake products that reduce mission readiness and lethality, and increase costs. We ensure products are authentic, verified, and validated. 
- Inventory - takes multiple warfighters to manage inventory manually =  takes a long time and mistakes are made. We reduce resources and time by 90% with no mistakes.

companies.name: Trackable Health AI
companies.warfareDomain: Air, Cyber, Information, Land, Multi-Domain, Sea, Space
companies.description: Trackable Health AI delivers a suite of AI products that transforms human performance oversight by pivoting from reactive care to proactive, real-time support, - a solution currently validated by the United States Air Force. Our hardware-agnostic platform, powered by Vantiq and hosted on AWS GovCloud, ingests non-interoperable data from diverse inputs (including SCIF-approved wearables like Garmin, Oura, and Whoop) to eliminate health "blind spots" between interactions and establish accurate precision medicine baselines to guide AI decision making. By providing gamification and user device choices to sustain engagement we deliver trustworthy, actionable data that enhances operational readiness while meeting strict enterprise compliance and security mandates. Deloitte and Vantiq provide the guardrails for the data to remain trustworthy and free from hallucinations.

Push notifications can allow systems to pivot from reactive to proactive support.

companies.name: SynapseMX
companies.description:
Problem
Aircraft readiness is critical to the DoD vision of a lethal, resilient, and adaptable force. Yet, legacy maintenance systems and outdated processes overburden maintainers and fail to deliver real-time, actionable data - directly impacting aircraft availability and National Defense Strategy readiness.

Solution
SynapseMX is a real-time workflow platform purpose-built for maintainers and QA teams. Using automation and AI, it increases aircraft availability and ensures QA compliance.

TRL & Validation
- TRL: 6
- AFWERX CSO SBIR Phase I
- Adapted by 164th & 189th AWs (Phase III)
- QA capability with NGB/A4M (Phase II)
- NGB/A4: Fielding in FY26 to 4 Wings via APB
- AFRC/A4: Fielding in FY26 via ARCWERX

Strategic Advantage
- Exclusive data access via ANG/A4M partnership
- First-mover timing edge: purpose-built maintenance/QA execution
- Building towards deep integrations (i.e BLADE, etc)
- Developed by seasoned maintenance professionals

Go-to-Market Access
- NGB/A4M connecting us to peers @ AMC, ACC, AFRC, HAF
- RSO MAC IDIQ

Dual-Use Potential
- Commercial airlines, 3rd-party MROs
- Maritime, fleet, and other high CapEx sectors

Team
- Shane Ballman: Head of maintenance innovation @ AirTran, core integration team w/ Southwest Airlines for FAA single operating certificate
- Katerina Barilov: UC Berkeley MBA, ex-Goldman Sachs aviation banker

Competitive Landscape
- LITE (via RSO), AIRS (Beacon), AireXpert, Beacon (Embraer-X)
- We’re different: purpose-built for Situational Awareness, Aircraft Availability, and QA compliance.

Primary Users
Work centers, Maintenance Control, Pro Supers, QA, MXG/CCs

User-Critical Problem
Disconnected systems and manual workflows erode morale, bury teams in inefficiency, and fail to deliver the data needed to meet mission readiness.

companies.name: Ulysses
companies.technologyArea: Advanced Computing and Software,Trusted AI / Autonomy,Integrated Sensing and Cyber
companies.description:
Fleets lack affordable, persistent surface-and-subsea coverage and ability to act; traffickers, saboteurs, and shadow assets are costing billions and risking lives. Western fleets have no current or upcoming solutions to match the volumes fielded by adversarial forces.

We build USVs and UUVs that are integrated and built to a cost profile that enables the delivery of 1000s of platforms instead of 100s, and have both sensors and data processing abilities, but also can take robotic payloads to interact with things on the seabed, in water column, and on surface.

Demos with ONR, and more commercial contracts across the US and Australia. We've deployed our AUVs commercially since late last year. 

USV TRL 5 - prototype tested, UUV TRL 7— prototypes proven at sea in multiple missions, docking autonomy entering TRL 6 this FY. Software Middleware TRL 4-5 

Vertically-integrated products, copy-paste manufacturing, scale-driven engineering drives costs to a point where we can deliver an order of magnitude more units than anyone else.

Active CRADA with NSWC; pursuing several DIU opportunities, teaming with primes e.g. SAAB

Energy (offshore wind, oil&gas), subsea telecom cables, port/coastal security, IUUF, restoration

Ex-McKinsey/Manna CEO, Ex-Manna/Spire CTO, Ex- Red Bull F1 Chief Engineer, 2 Navy veterans on team

Legacy primes (Kongsberg, L3, Leidos, HII, etc.) are generally single-domain and 10-100× costlier; New players e.g. Saildrone, Anduril, Saronic are building to usurp primes—no focus on delivering fleet-scale, dual-domain persistence and building the 1 of 0 capability we are developing.

Watch-stander / seabed-warfare lead. Commodores etc.

They juggle many sparse, low-confidence tracks, few, and costly field assets; Ulysses gives them many autonomous assets, enabling high-fidelity information to drive responses and interventions in a way that's not possible now.

companies.name: Airtrek Robotics
companies.description:
Problem: Preventable mishaps from poor wingwalking and incomplete FOD walks causes millions in repair cost and impact defense fleet readiness.

Solution: Airtrek's autonomous robot automate wingwalking and FOD walks, mitigating aircraft damage and freeing up service members for mission-critical tasks.

Field Validation: Tested in a civilian airport and hangar space; Not in a defense setting.

Technology Maturity (TRL): TRL 7 in the non-defense setting, tested in Lunken Airport (LUK) under heavy rain. 

Strategic Advantage: IP protection through a pending patent and a provisional application.

Go-to-Market Access: Existing connections with Air Force CMSgt., Lt. Col, AFLCMC/ROD, and InfiniteTactics that provides ATO'd secure cloud platform for multiple customers in the Air Force and Navy.

Dual-Use Potential: Aircraft ground operators in the non-defense market (e.g., FBO, airlines) face same challenges with manual wingwalking and FOD management, with estimated cost of $20 billion each year.

Team: Three co-founders specialize in autonomous systems and robotics with a track record of developing 10+ autonomous vehicles. Most notably, they successfully developed and deployed an autonomous airport baggage tractor at several US airports in their previous startup where they worked together. 

Competitive Landscape: A lidar-integrated towing machine (TowFlexx), autonomous platforms for FOD removal (Essential Aero, Coast Autonomous); Airtrek's multi-functional robot maximizes the dollar value and reduces operational complexity

Primary User: Aircraft Maintenance Squadrons (AMXS)

User-Critical Problem: Repetitive and time-consuming; Small mistake can cause significant damage.

companies.name: BKPK, Inc.
companies.description:
BKPK delivers immediate, clean, portable, and dependable energy anywhere, anytime, and at any scale. As tactical environments shift toward more dispersed and decentralized camps, warfighters and responders face growing risks to safety, readiness, and continuity from traditional fuel-based power systems. BKPK answers with rugged, single-person-portable battery systems that provide power when and where it’s needed — no noise, no fuel, no delay. Built to withstand harsh handling and extreme field conditions, each unit is drop-proof, waterproof (including its connectors), and engineered for reliable performance in any environment. The system is designed to be stupid-proof in the field—intuitive, tool-free, and resistant to common operator error, even under pressure. The modular packs work alone or in swarms, with composability from one to thousands, drawing from any source and adapting to mission needs with built-in edge intelligence.

Currently at TRL 5, BKPK’s Gen 5 units are undergoing lab testing and will enter field trials in Q3 2025 through a DLX-led zero-fuel camp pilot. Additional trials with military and emergency response teams are in planning. With over $45M in commercial MOUs and active partner pathways in solar, drone, and defense logistics, BKPK is scaling fast. Backed by 30+ IP vectors and a veteran team from NASA, Accenture, Daimler, Motorola, Flex, and Jabil, BKPK is not a science project — it’s a mission-ready energy platform built for defense, response, and the future of distributed power.

companies.name: Desert Rotor
companies.description:
Problem
Unmanned operations are fragmented. Air, land, and maritime platform often requires its own ground control system (GCS), creating training burdens, poor interoperability, and mission delays. In contested or denied environments, fragile communications and non-resilient networks increase operational risk.

Solution
Desert Rotor provides a single, interoperable GCS for all unmanned assets with redundant mesh networking and integrated 5G/SATCOM for resilient beyond-line-of-sight control. Currently advancing full multi-domain operations (MDO) capability.

Field Validation
550+ GCS deployed globally since 2020 with U.S. and allied defense and commercial operators. Proven in rugged environments with near‑zero downtime.

Technology Maturity
TRL 9 - Operationally proven hardware/software in production and fielded worldwide. Next-gen hardware in final pre-production.

Strategic Advantage
Proprietary cross‑platform HID/GCS stack independent of Windows/Linux/Android, patented integration workflows, unmatched interoperability, and 5+ years of deployment know‑how.

Go‑to‑Market
Direct DoD access (Army, Navy, DLA), contract vehicle companies, D2P2 SBIR awardee, expanding via SWMAC, client resellers

Dual‑Use
Energy, mining, environmental monitoring, disaster response, heavy‑lift, high‑altitude any robust unmanned control requirement.

Team
Debin Ray (CEO), Aaron Frank (CFO), Joe Burch (Principal Engineer), James Roe (VP Defense), Marc Chesley (Software Advisor).

Competitive Landscape
Competing with Tomahawk Robotics, UxV Technologies, Parry Labs, Mobilicom. Desert Rotor offers true platform‑agnostic control and MDO‑ready integration others lack.

Primary User
Frontline operators: ISR teams, C‑UAS crews, unmanned pilots. 

User‑Critical Problem
Multiple incompatible controllers and fragile comms slow missions, increase cognitive load, and limit operational flexibility

companies.name: Stitch3D
companies.description:
Problem
Traditional airfield inspections takes >8 hours; modern 3D laser scanning reduces capture to <1 hour. Yet LiDAR data remain difficult to access due to fragmented storage and outdated desktop software, delaying time-sensitive decisions in airfield recovery, terrain, and infrastructure assessments

Solution
Stitch3D is a cloud-native platform making 3D geospatial data usable on the web. Upload, visualize, analyze, and collaborate on 3D data with up to 50 users in real time, streamlining terrain workflows for faster, mission-critical decisions

Field Validation
Field-tested with 6 CES, 627 CES, and 628 CES. At JBLM, 627 CES used Stitch3D to share 1,000+ acres of drone LiDAR vegetation obstruction data with AMC A4, TERPS, 62 OSS, AFCEC, and the Army DPW

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 9. Commercial launch Jan 2024; MRR growing 25% MoM

Strategic Advantage
Our proprietary pipeline converts any LiDAR format into universal web format. Integrated hosting, visualization, and analysis tools in a powerful yet simple UI

Go-to-Market Access
7 total SBIR/STTR; signed MFR/LOS from 627 CES, 628 ABW, and AFCEC command leadership

Dual-Use Potential
Stitch3D serves 1,000+ commercial drone service providers across construction, utilities, and real estate

Team
Clark Yuan, CEO - former US Army intel officer. USMA Engineering; MIT and Harvard MBA/MPA
Jeff Jakinovich, CTO - former senior software engineer at Bosch

Competitive Landscape
Unlike ESRI and DroneDeploy, Stitch3D is cloud-native, intuitive, and LiDAR-capable at scale

Primary User
GeoMobility Coordinator, AMC/A4C; Geospatial Integration Manager, AFCEC/CBFG; PACAF Geospatial Integration Officer (GIO)

User-Critical Problem
Manual inspections strain CE teams. 3D models cut time, boost risk visibility, and optimize manpower

companies.name: Infinite Composites, Inc.
companies.description:
Problem
Increasing the capabilities of vehicles (satellites, hypersonics, UAVS, missile systems) by reducing the pressurized fuel storage weight, while providing more usable fuel storage space in the same footprint (or reducing the tank size).
Solution
Infinite Composites pressure vessels eliminate the metallic or plastic liner in composites overwrapped pressure vessels.
Field Validation
Customers under NDA. Tanks have been delivered for launch vehicles, lunar landers, torpedoes, satellites, & UAVs. Flight heritage in the categories of satellites, hypersonic aircraft, & hydrogen aircraft.
Technology Maturity
TRL 9- hypersonic flight, space flight propulsion
TRL 7 - comfortable tanks
TRL 6- cryogenic storage systems
Strategic Advantage
1 or 3 US companies that make this type of vessel. It's patented. Critical parts, such as specific material formulations, are confidential. 
Go-to-Market Access
Contracts with tier 1, or below prime contractors. Been awarded & completed SBIRs with the Army, NASA, & NSF. Been awarded by AFWERX & received signed MOUs from various labs & airwings.
Dual-Use Potential
Technology started in commercial natural gas vehicles & ground fueling storage. We've contracted with Formula1, BAJA racing, & Disney.
Team
Matt Villarreal & Michael Tate co-founded the company & have run it for 15 years. Jared Cuneo, Principal Engineer, former Chief of Structures at Firefly Space, & worked at Virgin Galactic. The team is heavy (50%) engineering.
Two direct competitors & numerous indirect competitors. Legacy indirect competitors include Northup Grumman & Raytheon.
Primary User
DevCom, Air Mobility Command, 375th AMW, 27 SOMXO, AFSOC, NasPax
User-Critical Problem
Missiles fly further/faster, UAVs fly longer & carry more payload, satellites stay in orbit longer.

companies.name: Ultraview AI
companies.technologyArea: Trusted AI / Autonomy,Advanced Computing and Software
companies.description:
The U.S. military operates a diverse fleet of aircraft, each requiring specialized tools and personnel for inspection. The Air Force is losing senior maintainers at a 12% annual rate, increasing pressure on remaining staff and contributing to a five-year high in Class A/B maintenance-related mishaps.
Ultraview’s autonomous drone platform performs full airframe inspections 80% faster than manual methods. It replaces clipboard walkarounds with high-res imagery, structured data capture, and automated reporting.
Deployments include Ascent Aviation Services for heavy and line maintenance. Warner Robins AFB allocated FY27 O&M funds and issued a Phase II customer memo for Ultraview operations starting September 2025 on the C-5, C-17, KC-130, and F-15.
Field-tested in commercial and defense settings, Ultraview delivers inspection-grade outputs and integrates into live MRO workflows.
Our patented platform uses LiDAR-based SLAM and ARK Flow for precise, GPS-denied navigation. It is fully self-contained and operates indoors or on flight lines with no added infrastructure.
We engage TPOCs directly, leverage SBIR Phase II via AFWERX, and connect with users through Ignite and Tradewinds to streamline procurement.
Ultraview supports defense and commercial aviation, UAM, and spaceport operations. It’s compatible with platforms from F-15s to A320s.
CEO Colby Harvey (ex-Google, RocketLab), COO Jack Medellin (ex-Lockheed F-35), and CPO Matt Kammerait (ex-AAR).
Boeing and Near Earth Autonomy lack a proven, autonomous product. Ultraview is field-validated, easy to operate, and deployable with no setup.
Primary User
Fleet maintenance leads, QA managers, and line chiefs.
User-Critical Problem
Tech shortages and manual methods slow readiness. Ultraview restores speed, accuracy, and consistency to inspection workflows.

CEO Colby Harvey (ex-Google, RocketLab), COO Jack Medellin (ex-Lockheed F-35), and CPO Matt Kammerait (ex-AAR).

companies.description:
Problem
Warfighters face detection risks from battlefield laser sensors. Multi-sensor solutions are bulky, complex, and computationally intensive.

Solution
We integrate camera features with multiple active sensors into a compact package, delivering image and depth mapping, as well as surface and material classification and identification.

Field Validation
We have been testing passive ranging with Softwerx and threat detection and ballistics with PEO Soldier..

Technology Maturity (TRL)
Our sensor is TRL9 and the device package is at TRL 7.

Strategic Advantage
We own 11 patents on technology for optically measuring electromagnetic waves and hold trade secrets on processing sensor output. The tech, developed over 25 years by our founder and CEO, is proprietary.

Go-to-Market Access
We currently active in several synergistic phase 1 and phase 2 SBIR programs.  We also have a PEO STRI contract for a program of record.  

Dual-Use Potential
We have several industrial customers funding our custom R&D and devices across sectors like industrial inspection, surveillance, security, and drone GIS. We're urgently targeting markets where we can replace LiDAR, as it demands high computational power and lacks complete image capture.

Team
Blair Barbour - ceo, inventor of spatial phase imaging and 11 related patents
Dave Truch - cto, seasoned professional from BP who led their innovation teams

Competitive Landscape
No one has been able to combine our feature set passively.

Primary User
Warfighters

User-Critical Problem
Ranging and accessing threats passively.

companies.name: Dunedain
companies.description:
Problem
Units burn critical hours on mapping, monitoring OSINT feeds, conducting MDMP, and checking doctrine from SOPs - in the fight this will often need to be offline and in a contested, high OPTEMP environment.

Solution
Warmind is a hybrid cloud / air-gappable AI platform that unifies geospatial analysis, OSINT ingestion, S1 / S2 / S3 artifact generation including MDMP products, and COP export into one portable system.

Field Validation
A prototype system has been tested with 19th SFG(A) in force on force exercises leading to a reported 400% acceleration in planning (orders, WARNO production and AAR synthesis). Multiple units that have ordered our system for purchase. Our unclassified open beta is also live now at beta.dunedainsystems.com with hundreds of monthly active users.

TRL
TRL 6 – Demonstrated prototype in real exercises and lab integration with TAK/COP tools.

Strategic Advantage
Deep doctrinal integration, modular unit and user-specific data retrieval, proprietary agent orchestration pipeline finetuned on Army data and built specifically for the Warfighter.

Go-to-Market
GPC-purchasable Warmind Laptops

Team
-Mack Ohlinger, CEO: West Point ’15, LBS MBA ’22, ex-Army Captain
-Matt Kane, CTO: West Point '15, Johns Hopkins MSE, former Signal Officer

Competitive Landscape
Unlike Camo GPT, Warmind is fully air-gapped and offers end-to-end agent workflows plus a flexible retrieval database enabling persistent unit and user memory enabling superior knowledge management and workflow outputs.

Primary User
S3 planners and S2 intel analysts who need rapid COP creation, rapid and accurate orders drafting and route planning in DDIL environments.

User-Critical Impact
Warmind delivers a ready-to-use operational artifacts in minutes, replacing fragmented tools and laborious manual staff-work associated with COP generation, staff work and other MDMP tasks.

companies.name: Hadron Industries
companies.description:
Problem
Army, Air Force and Navy network don't talk to each other. 

“Each of the services has systems to maintain situational awareness. Getting them to integrate and talk together, how the procedures [and] policies that different services use, when to display what, was really the problem.” -- Gen. John Murray (Commanding General, Army Futures Command)

Solution
Advanced Collaboration Enterprise Services (ACES) is an SBIR technology that provides an "I see what you see" experience that allows for many-to-many collaboration over disparate networks.  

Field Validation
- Deployed to TS/SCI facility (NRO NROC)
- Completed 2xMUAs and JCTD 

During the AWACS MUA, the Air Force documented that ACES reduced the time it took for ABMs to perform collaborative tasks by 20%. 

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 9. Can be deployed to INDOPACOM immediately. 

Strategic Advantage
Attempts to reverse engineer ACES over the last 10 years have failed. Not many companies are involved in multi-domain collaborations.

Go-to-Market Access
We've leveraged the AFWERX and SBIR Phase III contracts. Currently, on a 5-year/50 million SBIR Phase III contract with the IRS.
 
Dual-Use Potential
ACES is a good fit for any firms that requires many-to-many collaboration, distributed operations and distributed training.  
Team
Chief Engineer Klee Dienes: 
- MIT Software Engineer
- developed the prototype for the iPHONE
- MEDVAC Pilot and Cyber Officer 

Competitive Landscape
TEG and Creston do something similar, but are years behind ACES.
 
Primary User
The capabilities were developed via the SBIR program to connect the AOC/CRC with ABMs in the AWACS.  

User-Critical Problem
There is a lot of friction during collaboration between different networks. ACES reduces this friction by 20%. (See the ACES MUA)

companies.name: BestDefense, Inc
companies.technologyArea: Advanced Computing and Software,Integrated Sensing and Cyber
companies.description:
Problem
Most systems are still penetration tested manually and infrequently, leaving vulnerabilities undiscovered until it’s too late. Cyber Protection Teams and system owners need a way to emulate adversarial behavior to match the speed of modern threats.

Solution
AI-driven, autonomous penetration testing platform that identifies, prioritizes, and remediates vulnerabilities in apps, devices and infrastructure; red team coverage without waiting on contract cycles.

Field Validation
Pre-field. Internal pilots completed with commercial partners. Early partner interest from federal contractors. Adopted by TechStars as standard security tool for new SaaS startups.

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 6. Operational-scale testing on hardened cloud apps using AI-orchestrated open-source tooling. Active in commercial.

Strategic Advantage
Proprietary orchestration, chained exploit logic, and auto-remediation give Vortex an edge. A persistent adversary with fix generation. Built cloud-agnostic and VPC-ready.

Go-to-Market Access
Pursuing direct contracts, Techstars for Defense, and pilots with primes. Exploring AFWERX, DIU, and OTA teaming. Already in Carahsoft and Bridgepoint Technologies.

Dual-Use Potential
Applicable to critical infrastructure, healthcare, finance, and MSSPs, anywhere a breach could cripple operations.

Team
CTO Derek Foster led high-scale security programs across SaaS and fintech. CEO Dan has worked on FSISAC Threat Intelligence Repository, securing the NASDAQ.

Competitive Landscape
Qualys & Rapid7 lack continuous attack logic. Startups offer pen-test-as-a-service—but without automation or remediation. Vortex delivers both.

Primary User
Cyber Protection Teams, DevSecOps units (e.g., Platform One), and ATO system owners.

User-Critical Problem
Operators can’t run red teams on demand and are left guessing. Vortex resolves: autonomously, continuously.

companies.name: Helical Solar
companies.description:
Problem
Expeditionary Energy, particularly 15-60KW AMMPS generators in a contested logistics environment such as INDOPACOM can suffer from environmental induced failures, parts shortages, and fuel resupply disruptions.  Without continuous commercial grade power, modern military missions and the personnel that execute them are put in jeopardy.

Solution
With hot plug-and-play capability, we can seamlessly insert our high powered microgrid with an optimized dual-axis solar array between existing AMMPS generators and their loads to minimize run time and fuel consumption while also sustaining power during unplanned fuel shortages.

Field Validation
Prototype field tested at Tyndall AFB AFCEC (9700 Site).

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL = 7 (Tyndall AFCEC field prototype demonstration).

Strategic Advantage
2x U.S. Patents Issued and 1x Pending; Trade Secrets; Integration depth.

Go-to-Market Access
2024 NSIN Expeditionary Energy Challenge Finalist (Sponsor = 15th SPSS).

Dual-Use Potential
Agrivoltaics (solar and and agriculture/livestock) – 
$200K – DOE Phase I SBIR
$1.15M – DOE Phase II SBIR
$1.08M - USDA sponsored 122KWp World’s 1st In-pen Cattle Agrivoltaics at a Kansas Feedlot

Team
James McKinion (CEO/Founder) - Startups (Netspeed, CacheIQ), Tracor - Flight Test Engineer MQM-107E Drone. Fortune 500 - Cisco Systems, NetApp, Keysight.

Zach Hudnall - Design Engineer

Competitive Landscape
BoxPower, Southern Beams Builds, Sesame Solar, Beam Global – do not have a foldable, transportable dual-axis array or microgrid architecture that can allow “man-in-the-middle” insertion between generators and loads. 

Primary User
Electrical Power Production Specialist.

User-Critical Problem
No power = no comms, no edge compute, no environmental conveniences.

companies.name: High Entropy Security
companies.technologyArea: Integrated Sensing and Cyber,Quantum Science,Advanced Computing and Software
companies.description:
HES is focused on cybersecurity schemes for cryptographic key vulnerabilities in jammed, Zero-Trust environments. For terminal devices like UASs, the keys are vulnerable when they’re stored in memory or are transmitted through unsecured networks in data packets. With signal jamming, most UAS data packets are only able to handle about 20-25% bad bits before they become unusable for the tactical operators and mission analysts. The critical problem is ensuring usable data packets get through to UASs even with bad bits in the transmission. More usable packets mean lower latencies and smoother operation. Stronger cybersecurity around the keys is 100% needed. 

Our lightweight, quantum-resistant protocols (TRL 4) enable ground-to-terminal device key recovery in jammed/poor networks. The ephemeral keys are regenerated on demand, in real time, and never stored. Even with 45% bad bits in the transmission, the keys are error-free.

Co-founder Bertrand Cambou is an expert in physical unclonable functions, ternary computing, quantum key distribution, and device fingerprinting for secure authentication, with our methods protected by 80+ issued/pending patents. His experience includes Gemplus, Ingenico, Crocus, SST, AMD, and Motorola.

With foundational research funded by AFRL, we are funded by ARL’s Program FREEDOM and on track for field testing at Kostas Research Institute, championed by Sean Pascoli (ARL) and Donald Telesca (AFRL). We are going after SBIR/DIU/BAA opportunities and have partnered with defense consultant Alex Dely of Raytheon for contracts with primes and other funding. Our cyber schemes are applicable across public key infrastructures and device security, with dual-use in fintech and autonomous systems, including crypto wallet security with the ability to regenerate the private key as a service, as well as sensor cybersecurity for autonomous systems.

companies.name: Dmaterial IP, LLC.
companies.description: Combat medics, aircrews & vehicle operators lack scalable, on-demand CASEVAC & cargo solutions in contested environments. 85–95% of platforms—aircraft, watercraft, & ground vehicles—cannot move casualties or bulk cargo without rigid, pre-installed systems, resulting in dangerous delays & mission inflexibility. Rigid Inflatable Combat Systems (RICS) is a modular, inflatable system that rapidly converts any aircraft, boat, or ground vehicle into a high-capacity CASEVAC or cargo platform, doubling or tripling its usable capacity. It packs into a duffel bag, deploys without tools & integrates into the host platform without permanent modification. Selected for Army Futures Command’s Concept Focused Warfighting Experiment (CFWE), RICS is also engaged with SOFWERX, PD MEDEVAC, MECCD, & the Army Surgeon General’s Office. It is currently at TRL 6, with successful structural load testing, field inflation trials, & initial platform fitment completed. RICS is protected by patents & a proprietary RF-welding process that enables high-strength inflatable structures using UHMWPE-reinforced TPU composites. We’re entering the market through DoD experimentation, with a plan to advance to TRL 9 & begin direct sales via rapid acquisition pathways. Commercially, the same architecture will disrupt the $25B scaffolding industry with modular inflatable platforms that install 10–50× faster than rigid alternatives. My co-founder Warren Reasner has over 17 years of hands-on experience in RF-welding & inflatable systems design & manufacturing. Unlike rigid MEDVAC systems that are heavy, expensive, & vehicle-specific, RICS is universal, affordable, & field-deployable. Without RICS, operators resort to placing patients on the floor or strapping cargo in ad hoc ways—RICS fills that gap in minutes.

companies.description:
Problem
Current IP-based protocol TCP fails to meet the performance, latency, and reliability needs of modern mission-critical, distributed applications in wide-area networks (WANs). As AI, autonomous systems, and machine-to-machine operations scale, the inability to transfer large data volumes quickly and reliably across contested WANs creates strategic blind spots and operational risk.
Solution
a software-based, network-agnostic data transport solution with 10x throughput,10% latency of TCP/IP over existing WANs. Built for DDIL environments, it also supports enhanced security, LPD/LPI, and EMS signature management when integrated with network planning.
Field Validation
Being validated through lab emulation and real-world testing with government agencies, defense primes, and over 10 PoC with commercial partners. Actively engaged in four DoD SBIRs. Finalists in Army xTechSearch & ONR GlobalX. 
Technology Maturity
TRL 6.
Strategic Advantage
With over 90 years of combined experience, Genesis Codes owns proprietary algorithms for compression, fragmentation, and error correction. The open, modular architecture enables seamless integration. Our deep IP portfolio and trade secrets create high barriers to entry.
Go-to-Market
Our GTM strategy centers on partnerships, gov via SBIRs, Tier-1 defense primes, and commercial PoCs across various industries.
Dual-Use Potential
Beyond defense, commercial usage in energy, autonomy, edge-cloud, AI, finance, and satellite comm.
Team
Engineers and scientists from academia and top F500 firms, with technology, engineering, business, and entrepreneurship.
Competitive Landscape
Cisco, IBM, AWS offer partial solutions focused on normal networks. We stand apart with unique strength in DDIL.
Primary User: 
Military C5ISR

companies.name: Mythos AI
companies.description:
Naval operations are constrained by crew shortages and the demand for persistent maritime presence. While unmanned systems offer a solution, current platforms lack the autonomy maturity for safe, independent operation. 

Mythos AI provides a full-stack autonomy system for unmanned surface vessels (USVs).

Our software has logged over 10,000 autonomous miles across U.S. ports, operating in some of the busiest commercial waterways on commercial jobs. 

We are at TRL 9, with live deployments, commercial revenue, and field-proven integration with live sonar and navigation systems.

Built entirely in-house, our autonomy stack—from sensor drivers to decision logic—is purpose-built for the maritime domain. The software has been trained and validated on exclusive operational datasets from real-world autonomous missions in U.S. waterways. We deliver software that's both robust and scalable. We are hyper-focused entirely on the hardest problems.

We’re engaging directly with USV OEMs, pursuing PMS 420 alignment, and developing paths through SOFWERX and defense prime partnerships exploring small and medium USV solutions.

Our commercial-first strategy gave us maturity before entering defense, and our autonomy already supports dynamic navigation, object detection and avoidance, and hydrography.

Our team brings deep expertise from both the self-driving car industry and Amazon Robotics, combining automotive grade autonomy with industrial reliability.

Most of our competitors are busy building boats. Several of our competitors have now approached us to license our autonomy.  While others focus on hardware, we focus on software--the hardest part. 

Our primary users include Navy autonomy leads, fleet planners, and vessel commanders. They face overloaded watch teams and under-crewed ships. Mythos AI delivers the autonomy needed to expand operational reach without increasing risk.
