companies.name: Sentient Science
companies.description:
Problem
Thousands of military parts are candidates for transition to additive manufacturing, due to obsolescence or excessive lead times.  The main obstacle is qualifying the AM process.

Solution
DigitalClone physics-based modeling framework predicts the fatigue life performance of metals – and when integrated with AM machines enables layer-by-layer monitoring that can autonomously detect and correct defects to ensure part fatigue life and accelerate qualification.

Field Validation
SBIR Phase II (NASA JPL), SBIR Phase II (US Army DEVCOM AvMC), ongoing SBIR Phase II (AFWERX)

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 6 (results from Army Ph II:  25% less defects, 60% longer fatigue life vs parts printed in traditional AM process)

Strategic Advantage
Fatigue modeling relies on patented component life prediction (CLP) technology

Go-to-Market Access
Awarded 5-yr/$9M SBIR Phase III IDIQ (2023) from US Army Futures Command DEVCOM AvMC for DigitalClone enhancements
Access to Amazon Migration Acceleration Program (private cost-share up to $10M)

Dual-Use Potential
Automotive, Civil Aerospace, Energy, Industrial Equipment

Team
Jason Rios (Defense lead, USMA grad, MBA, former Honeywell)
Dr Nathan Bolander (CTO, PhD Purdue University)
Dr Behrooz Jalalahmadi (Additive Manufacturing VP, PhD Purdue University)
Dr Guru Madireddy (Additive Manufacturing Scientist, PhD Univ of Nebraska-Lincoln, formerly at Oak Ridge National Lab)

Competitive Landscape
AM machine manufacturers are able to monitor the print process, but no capability to autonomously detect and correct defects to ensure part fatigue life.

Primary User
Engineers (qualification) and DoD AM machine operators (printing)

User-Critical Problem
Lack of access to qualified AM parts magnifies part availability issues

companies.name: Confidencial Inc.
companies.technologyArea: Advanced Computing and Software,Integrated Sensing and Cyber,Trusted AI / Autonomy
companies.description:
Problem:
Perimeter defenses and network-based access controls remain vulnerable, as seen in high-profile breaches across government and industry. Sensitive data must flow between warfighters, mission partners, and AI agents, but current approaches rely on rigid enclaves or all-or-nothing access. This leads to overclassification and missed decision windows.

Confidencial enables secure, selective data sharing without enclaves. Our cryptographic protection embeds directly into unstructured data, enforcing usage controls across users, organizations, and AI - even after sharing. Users can redact, tokenize, or encrypt based on policy, with automatic enforcement.

Validation: 
The platform is live in financial services, legal, and healthcare, and has been demonstrated through AFWERX for the AF SABRE team. It supports cloud, on-prem, and disconnected use, and integrates with AI systems. 

TRL is 6 for commercial deployments and nearing TRL 5 for defense.

Strategic Edge: Confidencial uniquely enables data-centric security aligned with Zero Trust and ABAC principles.

GTM: Our go-to-market leverages traction with SIs, VARs, and strong demand in enterprise and AI governance. Customers include Lilly, the NFL, and major law firms.

Dual Use: The tech supports dual-use across DoD, healthcare, and finance where sensitive data must be controlled, shared, and audited.

Team: The team includes cryptography researchers from SRI and other defense contractors. 

Competition: Most alternatives stop at labeling, audit, or perimeter enforcement - we enforce policy cryptographically, at the data layer.

User and problem: Primary users are mission planners and analysts facing daily friction coordinating time-sensitive, sensitive data, using AI and other tools with multi-national coalitions.

companies.name: LeVanta Tech Inc.
companies.description:
The Pacific is large.  A need exists for real-time/near-real-time cross-domain maritime data.

The HALIA float-and-fly drone is a persistent sea-air interface capable of high-speed flight with low detectability.

Field testing is ongoing in the Gulf of America and Mississippi Sound.

TRL 6: field trials - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=o2KA2-2oSIg (we have ongoing testing almost weekly - please contact us for the latest updates)

Currently, the only real-time data solutions are buoys, gliders, boats, planes, and helicopters. Buoys are fixed in location, gliders and boats are slow, and planes and helicopters are too expensive for continuous data acquisition. Existing aerial drones either have limited endurance or cannot land and takeoff on the ocean in rough conditions. The HALIAs IP-protected wings, Hoverfoils, enable rough sea float.   

We have a Direct-to-Phase 2 SBIR with the Air Force and have won a Direct-to-Phase 2 SBIR with the Navy.

Critical infrastructure protection, illegal fishing monitoring, and environmental monitoring

Kelly Echols, Founder and CEO.  Engineer and patent attorney.
Johnny Doo, CTO - 40 years of experience designing aircraft.  
Jon Smith, Director of UAS - Test Program Manager for several U.S. Navy UAS test programs.  
Dave Markert, Military Advisor F-14 pilot, Top Gun Instructor, and former Head of ONR Global London.  

Tube-launched UAS from buoys and USVs are one attempt to solve the problem we are solving; however, tube-launched UAS cannot land on the ocean again post-launch.  The HALIA can fly-float-fly.  Tethered UAS can return to their original launch site (with wind limitations); however, the tethered UAS cannot rapidly relocate to new positions.  That is one advantage the HALIA has over tube-launched and tethered UAS.

NAVO, SOCOM, MDA

The HALIA can provide a comms relay for submerged assets, avoiding their need to surface.

companies.name: Rune Aero
companies.description:
In contested, infrastructure-limited environments like island chains, warfighters lack a scalable way to move critical cargo. Traditional aircraft require long runways and fuel convoys. That logistics footprint is vulnerable and unsustainable. In some cases, it takes 7 gallons of fuel to deliver one. Rune Aero is developing an autonomous, low-noise hybrid-electric cargo aircraft capable of short takeoff and landing. It’s designed to deliver meaningful payloads where large transports can’t go and small UAVs can’t carry enough while reducing fuel consumption.
Pre-field. We’re conducting all-electric subscale prototype flight testing, and are preparing for hybrid-electric propulsion (HEP) hardware development, and flight testing. 
Currently at TRL 4 with working subscale systems and NASA-backed design and modeling efforts under multiple SBIR grants.
Our advantage lies in our aircraft-level configuration and systems integration - combining STOL aerodynamics, HEP, and scalable autonomy from the ground up. IP covers HEP architecture and configuration.
We’re engaging NASA, AFWERX, and direct DoD stakeholders to access field programs. On the commercial side, we’re targeting middle-mile logistics and time-sensitive medical transport.
Our team includes aerospace engineers and PMs from Bombardier, Boeing, Lockheed Martin, McKinsey, and Amazon Prime Air.
Unlike VTOLs or autonomy retrofits, Rune is purpose-built for contested logistics - balancing range, payload, and footprint like no one else.
Primary users: Air Mobility Command, expeditionary logistics officers, Marine Littoral Regiments, and SOCOM sustainment teams - whose daily challenge is delivering mission-critical cargo without relying on fuel convoys or fixed infrastructure.

companies.name: HI-Spectral
companies.description:
– Traditional high-perf snapshot Hyperspectral Imaging (HSI) systems are large, fragile, and slow, making them impractical for tactical or space-limited platforms.
– HI-SNAP is a game-changing, compact (shoebox size), rugged HSI unit capturing hundreds of spectral bands in a single shot — no scanning. Its small size enables deployment on drones, CubeSats, manned aircraft, or other low-SWaP assets for the first time.
– Originally dev’d w/ Canon Japan for DKIST, a $400M solar observatory. Two systems are deployed and operational. Gen-2 units tested in lab under op conditions and are fully ready for deployment. Space-ready versions are in pre-field stage.
– Gen-1 is TRL 9. Gen-2 is TRL 7, lab-tested. Space-config version is TRL 5–6, w/ validated components in relevant environments.
– Miniaturizing the image slicer enabled shrinking the full system w/o perf loss. Our slicer is 50× smaller & 3× denser than JWST’s, using all-metal optics. Canon is pursuing IP licensing for future production.
– Pursuing SBIR funding, working w/ defense primes, and engaging users via INDOPACOM, xTech, etc.
– Dual-use potential: precision ag, env monitoring, semicon inspection, med imaging, and remote sensing. Canon is evaluating a molded commercial version.
– Haosheng Lin, PhD, CEO: Astronomer w/ 35+ yrs in advanced telescope instrumentation. Morgan Bonnet, MS, COO: Optomechanical engr w/ 20 yrs in academia & industry.
– Others use scanning or filters w/ trade-offs in resolution or speed. Existing high-perf grating-based snapshot HSI systems are larger than a fridge.
– Primary users: ISR teams, missile defense analysts, SDA ops using UAVs, CubeSats, or ground systems.
– Current systems are too large for small, mobile, or tactical platforms — limiting access to real-time spectral intel.

companies.name: Kindo
companies.description:
Problem:  
Cyber is the front line. Adversaries exploit every gap, legacy tool, and manual process. The DoD can’t fight tomorrow’s battles with yesterday’s software.

Solution:  
Kindo lets you command in plain English—“Scan for threats. Lock down this network.” No technical hoops. With Chat Actions, you state your mission; Kindo does the rest.

Field Validation:  
Not yet fielded in DoD. At Aireon, our platform identified and repelled a Salt Typhoon attack using Deep Hat.

Technology Maturity (TRL):  
5, prior to DOGE.

Strategic Advantage:  
We launched before OpenAI came out of stealth. No Langchain or Agent SDK existed then. Our agent can self-install and use tools—an industry first. We’re also the only commercial AI platform that can be self-hosted.

Go-to-Market Access:  
We’re a Series A company with three years in commercial markets, now pursuing DoD work. Our founder and CEO, Ron Williams, is former AF; our team has deep defense and intel experience.

Dual-Use Potential:  
We have commercial customers and are ready to create a defense version with funding.

Team:  
Ron Williams (CEO, former AF, 3x CISO), Bryan Vann (CTO, architected Google Drive), Andy Monaske (VP Product, former AF intel, first Hashicorp Vault engineer), Ken Kato (CSO, founding cloud architect for USAF Kessel Run, former White House Fellow).

Competitive Landscape:  
We’re AI native with features OpenAI and Anthropic lack. Often compared to Torq, but we’re not just a security company.

Primary User:  
Industries: hospitality, chip manufacturing, air traffic management, and more.

User-Critical Problem:  
Kindo collapses tool sprawl and automates grunt work, so teams focus on outcomes. Self-hosted, secure, and compliant—Kindo delivers speed and control without lengthy onboarding.

companies.name: Modular Misfits
companies.description:
We’ve got warfighters waiting 12 minutes for fire support during a firefight because systems can’t talk across services, partners, or platforms. That’s not latency, that’s failure. It’s costing lives and letting targets walk. The kill chain is broken where it matters most, at the edge, across the Indo-Pacific.

The Strike Harmonization And Fires Toolkit (SHAFT) is the “Fires Officer in a Box” a software-only, composable system (what MOSA compliant means) that ingests targeting data, validates ROE, and pushes fully formed strike requests across service formats and coalition networks before the target relocates.

Pre-field, but built by a team that’s delivered operational tech to DoD, IC, and the Pentagon. Units like 613th AOC in PACAF and 5th ANGLICO at MARFORPAC are calling for live-fire integration now.

SHAFT is TRL 5, the TAK plug-in functional, fire missions output as AFATDS and CoT, stress-tested over degraded SATCOM, TSM mesh, and intermittent networks, with embedded ROE logic.

We don’t need new radios, fragile SaaS, or another $100M integration effort. SHAFT runs on what’s already deployed, speaks every fires dialect, and doesn’t wait on a JROC-approved fantasy.

We’re fielding through OTA, CSO, DIU, or straight to units with urgent needs via partners like BAE, Amentum, and Northrop.

Dual-use? Think HA/DR ops in the Pacific, drone deconfliction, and real-time C2 during blackouts.

I’m Tony Maida, Head Misfit at Modular Misfits. Our coalition, TORII, Colvin Run, Fraclex, KForm, Akima, HPE, IBM, BAE, Northrop, is a kill chain kill squad.

While primes sell theory, we ship reality. MVP in 60 days, mission-ready under continuous ATO in 12 months for $2–4M. JTACs, TACPs, and FSOs don’t need more talk. They need SHAFT now before they get shafted.

Pre-field, but built by a team that’s delivered operational tech to DoD, IC, and the Pentagon. Units like 613th AOC in PACAF and 5th ANGLICO at MARFORPAC are calling for live-fire integration now.

SHAFT is TRL 5, the TAK plug-in functional, fire missions output as AFATDS and CoT, stress-tested over degraded SATCOM, TSM mesh, and intermittent networks, with embedded ROE logic.

We don’t need new radios, fragile SaaS, or another $100M integration effort. SHAFT runs on what’s already deployed, speaks every fires dialect, and doesn’t wait on a JROC-approved fantasy.

We’re fielding through OTA, CSO, DIU, or straight to units with urgent needs via partners like BAE, Amentum, and Northrop.

Dual-use? Think HA/DR ops in the Pacific, drone deconfliction, and real-time C2 during blackouts.

I’m Tony Maida, Head Misfit at Modular Misfits. Our coalition, TORII, Colvin Run, Fraclex, KForm, Akima, HPE, IBM, BAE, Northrop, is a kill chain kill squad.

While primes sell theory, we ship reality. MVP in 60 days, mission-ready under continuous ATO in 12 months for $2–4M. JTACs, TACPs, and FSOs don’t need more talk. They need SHAFT now before they get shafted.

companies.name: Redshred
companies.technologyArea: Advanced Computing and Software,Trusted AI / Autonomy
companies.description:
Problem
In 2025 mission capable rates for aircraft fell to their lowest in recent memory: 62%

A surprising contributor to this was paper. Not even physical paper, but its digital counterparts like PDFs. Despite being digital, the knowledge in the diagrams, tables, charts and instructions within are still not easily accessible from software systems.

Solution
Redshred provides an API-first document AI platform for digital transformation and data readiness.  that lets you dial in exactly what you need from these documents and turn it into the structured data you need to power downstream applications.  Redshred lets you treat your documents as a database.

Field Validation
Commercial customers; Air Mobility Command; DAF Rapid Sustainment Office; USMC PM AAA; Navy FRCSW

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 7+

Strategic Advantage
Patents and patents pending; subject matter expertise in aviation maintenance.

Go-to-Market Access
SBIRs including AFWERX, DARPA, Navy, and Army and is on the RO-MAC IDIQ and Tradewinds.

Dual-Use Potential
Commercial MRO and engineering; digital transformation across an array of industries

Team
Redshred's founders have run multiple businesses and have a diverse past including prior startup engineering experience at Sourcefire.  Team includes multiple PhDs, software engineers, and data scientists as well as maintenance subject matter experts.

Competitive Landscape
Manual conversion, bespoke software development, OCR companies and general purpose LLMs like ChatGPT.  Redshred’s unique advantage is choreographing many layers of AI, synthesizing their results, and providing a database-like interface via APIs.

Primary User
Maintainers and engineers.

User-Critical Problem
Modernized access to tech data supporting maintenance training and operations.

companies.name: RelayNexus
companies.description:
Problem
Relay Nexus addresses the critical limitations and exposure in underwater communication and navigation currently faced by both private and military sectors

Solution
A laser communication system capable of transmitting a bi-directional beam using adaptive optics and an algorithm to correct alignment of the beam. 

Field Validation
Initial testing done at China Lake California Optics Team at Naval Air Warfare Weapons Division. Additional research partner established at University of Central Florida CREOL for extension of signal transference. 

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 4 lab demonstration with use of adaptive optics successfully captured reflected signal, proving a bi-directional signal, also completed detection signatures from an underwater objects. 

Strategic Advantage
Use of specific lasers allows for frequency dithering and frequency-hopping, which can make laser communications harder to detect/intercept, offering low probability of detection/intercept. 

Go-to-Market Access
DHS, NAVSEA

Dual-Use Potential
The technology establishes tetherless signal transference enabling wireless comms for subsurface vehicles to transmit signals through bi-directional lasers without surfacing.

Team
Ari Candelier (CEO)
Ivan Divliansky (Research and Development) 
Nicholas Guilbeault (CTO)
Zain Shams (Managing Director)

Potential competitors include HydroNet, LumaX, but RelayNexus is in a unique position with the use of laser optics as primary communication channel. 

Primary User
Dual-Use Remotely Operated Vehicles working with government contracts related to mine hunting and coastal security. 

User-Critical Problem
Exposure to adversaries when using acoustics (even when encrypted) in contested environments increases risks of possible loss of life and technology. Also, use of tethered systems limit distance and mobility.

companies.name: Leviathan Air
companies.description:
Problem

Attrition of distance.  Transporting missions specific payloads and cargo long distances over oceans is a massive challenge, especially if needed to be done fast.    

Solution

We are building remote operated semi-autonomous Ground Effect Vehicles (GEVs) to provide long range high-speed transport of equipment and cargo across oceans while being able to takeoff and land on the water.  

Field Validation

Pre-field 

Technology Maturity (TRL)

TRL 4 - Building our first flying prototype 

Strategic Advantage

We are building large size, complex to design, flying machines used in a marine environment.  Everything about what we are doing is hard and expensive.  Us being one of the first players in this space will give us a massive head start in the long run.  

Go-to-Market Access

SBIR/STTRs to start followed by pursuing DIU and direct contracts with Navy, Marines, and SOCOM.  

Dual-Use Potential
[Where does this extend commercially? Highlight urgent markets: energy, logistics, AI safety, etc.]

Interisland cargo transport and carrying inspection drones to offshore installations like wind turbines and oil & gas.  Larger versions long term can do transoceanic heavy cargo transport.

Team

Riley Knox (CEO) - Mechanical Engineer, 2nd time founder
Scott Rewerts (CTO) - Aerospace Engineer, Head of Aerodynamics at Honda Jet, Head of Aerodynamics at Eclipse Aviation, Head of Flight Sciences at Radia (https://radia.com/)

Competitive Landscape

Regent & Poseidon Aerospace
 
Primary User

Navy, Marines, and SOCOM using our GEVs for ISR, ASW, E Warfare, Tactical Resupply, & CUAS.  

User-Critical Problem

Commanders can send GEVs to patrol dangerous remote areas areas with no risk to pilots.    Special Forces in need of resupply will be able to have 1000lbs of equipment delivered to remote islands at aircraft speeds.

companies.name: Motibera, Inc.
companies.description:
Problem
Warfighters rely on electric motors and generators, but these often fail from bearing wear, oil breakdown, and shock — especially in harsh or denied environments. Failures reduce uptime, raise logistics, and weaken energy resilience.

Solution
Motibera makes compact levitating motors "Motibera Halo" that remove friction, wear, and oil using magnetic forces — enabling reliable, maintenance-free operation in austere conditions.

Field Validation
Pre-field. Lab demos and prototypes validated magnetic stabilization under varying load and speed.

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 3–4. Core subsystems, including magnetic suspension with multi-DOF control, are validated. Advancing toward integrated MIL-spec prototypes.

Strategic Advantage
Our motor combines levitation and drive in one unit, reducing parts and volume. Unlike bolt-on systems, it’s compact, oil-free, and scalable. We hold foundational IP (patent pending) and are developing proprietary controls and TEA models.

Go-to-Market Access
Pursuing AFWERX Phase I and exploring DIU. Targeting DoD integrator partners.

Dual-Use Potential
Also fits HVAC, energy, and mobility — ideal for oil-free chillers, drones, and industrial drives.

Team
Led by an Activate Fellow (PhD) with expertise in motor design, control, and power electronics.

Competitive Landscape
Legacy systems are bulky, costly, or underpowered. Incumbents use passive or bolt-on mag bearings. We’re building a unified, low-cost, maintenance-free motor from scratch.

Primary User
Electrification leads, expeditionary power PMs, and planners for austere HVAC, drones, and mobile power — also relevant to unmanned logistics and tactical energy teams.

User-Critical Problem
Deployed systems need frequent bearing and oil maintenance, raising risk and downtime. Our motors eliminate this — extending missions, cutting resupply, and improving uptime.

companies.name: Flagged Inc.
companies.description:
Problem
We are tackling the escalating threat of AI-powered cyberattacks, including phishing, business email compromise (BEC), social engineering scams, and zero-day vulnerabilities. These attack methods, often deployed by sophisticated state-backed actors, are designed to breach and disrupt mission-critical operations in both public and private sector systems. Legacy cybersecurity tools are increasingly ineffective against these evolving threats, putting sensitive data, operations, and financial assets at high risk.

Solution
Our platform leverages advanced artificial intelligence and machine learning algorithms to detect, flag, and mitigate threats across communication channels in real time. 

Field Validation
Our technology is currently live and being deployed across the United States, with ongoing tests in collaboration with several small and mid-sized enterprises (SMEs).

Technology Maturity
We are in live deployment. Organizations can access the enterprise-grade version at https://www.getflagged.net

Strategic Advantage
-Exclusive access to data
-In-house AI/ML expertise
-First-mover in AI-native cyber threat
Go-to-Market Access
Partnerships
Dual-Use Potential
Our solution has broad applications in finance, energy, logistics, technology, and defense sectors. With $55.5B lost to cybercrime in the U.S. alone in 2024, the urgency is clear.

Team
Led by  Professor Kanayo Ogwu (CEO) and Professor Mohsen Moslehpour (CTO), the team brings experience from Meta, Cruise, Yahoo, AOL, and academia, supported by cybersecurity engineers and data scientists.

Users
Primary users include CISOs, CIOs, ITPSOs, and cyber command centers  facing real-time cyber risk.

companies.name: EM Sektet
companies.description:
Problem — Mission‑critical / market‑critical
Satellites die when propellant runs out. Every collision‑avoidance burn and station‑keeping maneuver consumes finite Δv, shortening constellation life, forcing costly replenishment, and leaving assets exposed to ASAT threats. Responsive space adds mass, complexity, and schedule risk from pressurized tanks and fuel logistics, resulting in rationed maneuvers, reduced agility, and premature deorbiting.

Solution — One‑sentence core
EM Sektet is a low‑voltage, propellantless electromechanical “momentum thruster” for station‑keeping, collision avoidance, and fine maneuvering, extending mission life and enabling higher maneuver cadence.

Field Validation
	•	Pre‑flight: Lab/bench demos with air‑bearing and water‑surface motion (2024).
	•	Interest: Spacecom letter of support (Oct 2024).
	•	Next: 1U CubeSat space demo post‑Phase II.

Technology Maturity (TRL 4)
	•	<200 g prototype produced visible net thrust; scale‑ups to ~10 N on 5 lb platform; 30→9 V; ~10 W; embedded “repeater” electronics.

Strategic Advantage
	•	Proprietary Sektet Coil + momentum harness + drive electronics; hard to reverse‑engineer.
	•	Scalable, low‑voltage tiling for higher net thrust.
	•	Timing edge: first credible propellantless space demo secures data and integration lead.

Dual‑Use Potential
	•	Space: station‑keeping, formation‑flying, de‑tumbling.
	•	Marine/robotics

Team
	•	Ed Chen, inventor; led multi‑scale builds, power reduction, embedded controls.

Competitive Landscape
	•	Hall/ion thrusters need propellant; EM Sektet is modular, power‑limited, and propellant‑free.

Primary User / Pain
	•	Constellation flight‑dynamics leads ration burns, handle hazardous propellants, and need more Δv without fuel to extend mission life and simplify bus integration.

companies.description:
Problem
Social Engineering still accounts for 98% of all cyber attacks, AI makes these attacks more effective.

Solution
Our solution uses detection models to stop or flag social engineering attempts, and our models work with email, sms, or voice personas. Our offensive models can hold conversations, continue to pry for information or deliver a payload, and can communicate with other personas to share information to adapt.

Field Validation
We are working with the Air Force Academy to validate multi-lingual models, and mission partners in the DoD.

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 6 - We are commercially available today, with a prototype on DoD networks.

Strategic Advantage
Our models continuously improve through red-team vs blue-team simulation loops, and the only platform that using darkweb data to utilize the same data that malicious actors use.

Go-to-Market Access
We currently are finishing up a Phase 2 STTR and TACFI with AFWERX.

Dual-Use Potential
Jericho provides defensive models and training to customers in the financial, health, and insurance sectors.

Team
CEO: Sage Wohns - Former CEO of Agolo
CTO: Dan Chyan - CSSP, seasoned CTO
PM: Keith Perez - Former CIO at Naval Health Clinic Charleston
Fed Tech Lead: Tony Giaimo - Former Cyber Warfare Officer (17S)

Competitive Landscape
Microsoft and proof-point provide some level of email security, but they don't cover sms, voice, and deepfake attack avenues.

Primary User
Defense models used by comm squadron technicians at base comms and air operations centers.
Offensive models used by initial access cyber mission teams and information warfare officers

User-Critical Problem
CMT operators don't have the resources to carry out successful multi-channel campaigns, especially not in adversaries native languages.

companies.name: Sunnoo
companies.description:
In modern forward-operating environments, the military’s reliance on diesel generators and fuel convoys creates serious vulnerabilities. Fuel convoys are among the most dangerous missions in the field, while generators are loud, hot, and maintenance-heavy — emitting detectable signatures that expose troops to enemy targeting. With electrification increasing across drone fleets, sensor platforms, and mobile comms, the battlefield demands a new standard of portable, resilient power.

Sunnoo delivers that standard. Our mobile, solar-powered microgrid provides silent, thermal-signature-free energy without the need for fuel. It eliminates logistical risk, reduces downtime, and enables mission continuity in contested environments — keeping warfighters powered, protected, and operational.

Key features include a 12kW expandable solar array with patented dual-axis sun tracking — delivering 42% more solar capture than static systems — plus scalable battery storage (480kWh–1,440kWh), daisy-chain expandability, and a rugged, trailer-mounted design for fast deployment. Optional cloaking technology visually blends the unit with its terrain, enhancing stealth in high-threat zones.

Sunnoo is validated by 20,000+ hours of component testing and DOE partnerships via Sandia Labs and New Mexico State. At TRL 6, our platform is ready to move from field pilot to deployment. companies.synopsisRaw:
In modern forward-operating environments, the military’s reliance on diesel generators and fuel convoys creates serious vulnerabilities. Fuel convoys are among the most dangerous missions in the field, while generators are loud, hot, and maintenance-heavy — emitting detectable signatures that expose troops to enemy targeting. With electrification increasing across drone fleets, sensor platforms, and mobile comms, the battlefield demands a new standard of portable, resilient power.

Sunnoo delivers that standard. Our mobile, solar-powered microgrid provides silent, thermal-signature-free energy without the need for fuel. It eliminates logistical risk, reduces downtime, and enables mission continuity in contested environments — keeping warfighters powered, protected, and operational.

Key features include a 12kW expandable solar array with patented dual-axis sun tracking — delivering 42% more solar capture than static systems — plus scalable battery storage (480kWh–1,440kWh), daisy-chain expandability, and a rugged, trailer-mounted design for fast deployment. Optional cloaking technology visually blends the unit with its terrain, enhancing stealth in high-threat zones.

Sunnoo is validated by 20,000+ hours of component testing and DOE partnerships via Sandia Labs and New Mexico State. At TRL 6, our platform is ready to move from field pilot to deployment. We’ve submitted multiple SBIR applications and built a go-to-market roadmap focused on DoD energy resilience needs.

With dual-use potential for disaster relief and EV infrastructure, Sunnoo offers not just a cleaner energy alternative — but a safer, smarter one that aligns with the modern warfighter’s reality. Built in the U.S.A. and deployable worldwide, it’s energy engineered for survivability and strategic advantage.

companies.name: AEROSENS
companies.description:
Problem
Critical operations in commercial aviation and defense rely on outdated processes using paper checklists and human reporting. These methods are slow, error-prone, and lack real-time visibility. This causes mistakes, and delays missions. As aviation and defense demand faster readiness, and digital traceability, traditional methods can’t keep pace. 

Solution
AEROSENS’ sensor-enabled platforms (AeroCMS and AeroISU) automate readiness checks for aircraft equipment and provides in-transit visibility and tamper detection for cargo, replacing blind manual processes with verifiable digital certainty.

Field Validation
AeroISU: AFWERX Phase II, under TACFI with the 621st CRG.
AeroCMS:  AFWERX Phase I and pilots with British Airways and JetBlue.

Technology Maturity (TRL)
Both platforms are at TRL 6

Strategic Advantage
AeroCMS is patented and FAA/FCC/CE certified, with deep workflow integration. AeroISU has a patent-pending ultra-secure BLE sensor. Years of validation give us timing, compliance, and integration advantages

Go-to-Market Access
Advancing through AFWERX SBIR, TACFI, and OTA consortia . Commercial sales via HEICO and direct channels, backed by Boeing portfolio selection

Dual-Use Potential
Global logistics, energy, rail, and emergency response

Team
Maria Martinez (CEO) – Telecoms Eng., ESADE MBA
Reza Shibli (CTO) – CS, McGill University

Competitive Landscape
RFID firms like ASD offer only proximity scans. Defense players like Savi and SkyBitz require connectivity and complex infrastructure

Primary User
AeroISU: AMC, CRG, AIT/ITV
AeroCMS: Fleet Maintenance Heads

User-Critical Problem
Manual tracking leads to missed inspections and lost visibility. Poor tools and training cause delays and misreporting.

companies.name: NetCentrics Corporation
companies.description:
Problem
Mission teams in distributed, contested, hybrid, or classified environments lack real-time, enriched cyber visibility—causing delayed threat detection, analyst overload, and degraded mission assurance.
Solution
Wraith is a cloud-native telemetry fusion and enrichment platform delivering mission-critical insights in disconnected, denied, hybrid, and distributed environments.
Field Validation
Deployed with the Air National Guard, supporting two Cyber Protection Teams (CPTs).
Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 7 — Operational in DoD, including JWICS/SIPR, supporting ANG CPT missions.
Strategic Advantage
Leverages NetCentrics’ classified access to ingest unique telemetry, integrates with Sentinel and Splunk, and operates where traditional tools fail—disconnected and multi-domain.
Go-to-Market Access
DIU via SigmaBlox, NetCentrics IDIQs (DISA, INDOPACOM, USCG, VA), SBIR, and SOC/SOAR roles.
Dual-Use Potential
Energy (OT/ICS telemetry), Logistics (contested edge), and AI Safety (model monitoring).
Team
Kenny Cushing (CEO), Steve Wieland (CTO), Ashish Francis (VP Data/AI), Brian Dutcher (Chief Architect), plus Chaney, Long, Fecteau, Nalls—experts from USAF, Guard, USCYBERCOM, OSD R&E, and Intel.
Competitive Landscape
Unlike Vectra, MixMode, Leidos, or Booz Allen, Wraith spans classification levels (NIPR/SIPR/JWICS), functions offline, and enriches telemetry pre-SIEM/SOAR.
Primary User
Cyber analysts, SOC leads, and operators in classified/multi-domain ops.
User-Critical Problem
Analysts face siloed, noisy telemetry and offline gaps. Wraith fuses reliable insights across cloud and on-prem—without persistent connectivity.

companies.name: O Analytics
companies.technologyArea: Trusted AI / Autonomy,Advanced Computing and Software
companies.description:
Problem: Limited real-time capability to detect, classify and monitor not-before-seen space objects.

Solution: Patent-pending detection and characterization algorithms as part of a comprehensive Space Object Custody Management System (SOCMS). 
Field Validation: Pre-field

Technology Maturity (TRL): 6 | Components tested on real world sensor datasets.

Strategic Advantage: Patent-pending image processing code enables precision (i.e., accurate) real-time (i.e., fast) performance and low bandwidth required results (i.e., efficient) autonomously (i.e., AI/ML-enabled) from the edge (i.e., space- or ground-based).

Go-to-Market Access: Primarily AFWERX.

Dual-Use Potential: SOCMS can be extended to sovereign ally countries and commercial satellite constellation operators. 

Team: Our team is led by an award-winning computational scientist with extensive experience in defense systems sensors, signal and image processing, AI/ML and technology management. Our exceptional team of computer science, physics, machine learning, sensor phenomenology, data science, space intelligence and non-Earth imagery (NEI) experts include a number of patent holders (i.e., innovators), and have 40+ combined years of defense, commercial aerospace, and government R&D experience.

Competitive Landscape: ExoAnalytics, Slingshot Aerospace, Kratos, LeoLabs and others offering a single sensor phenomenology are hardware dependent/limited. Our solution is sensor agnostic and fuses space object data from multiples sensor sources in real-time.  

Primary User: Space Systems Command and combatant commands.

User-Critical Problem: The ultimate pain point is the unacceptable likelihood that a bad actor (i.e., missile) could penetrate space without detection and consequently, without adequate time to respond.

companies.name: Carolina Unmanned Vehicles Inc.
companies.description:
Problem
In peer conflict Forward deployed units require low cost, survivable, and mobile systems for 24/7 wide area Surveillance, C3, Electronic Warfare, and Counter-UAS capability. 

Solution
CUVs Expeditionary Aerostat System uses the a tethered blimp combining helium and wind lift.  Small sizes operate in high wind, allowing small mobile systems. EAS aerostats are mobile, air transportable, minimum manpower and low cost. It has lower air transport, logistics and manpower requirements than aircraft, or UAS. Coverage has Line of Sight to 100 kilometers

Field Validation
Early iteration deployed to small Forward Operating Bases in Afghanistan 

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 9. Live deployment. 

Strategic Advantage
Mature design developed over 15 plus years. 

Go-to-Market Access
Developed under SBIR, followed by Army Rapid Equipping Force contracts, and contracts from Lockheed and others. Currently marketing to Army C5ISR Center, NATO allies. 

Dual-Use Potential
Homeland security border control, remote area security (mines, refinery, etc.) Post disaster communication

Team
Project manager, Mike Rogers, retired USAF LTC. Over 50 years R and and development experience. 

Competitive Landscape
Somewhat similar systems produced by Israel and other allies. 

Primary User
Army Brigade and above for distributed Command Posts being developed. Missions for C3, electronic warfare, intelligence collection, surveillance. 

User-Critical Problem
Command posts are targeted by enemy reconnaissance systems and will be attacked in peer conflict. Only by dispersal and concealment can they survive, requiring long range, mobile communications. This requires lifting payloads hundreds of meters to obtain long Line of Sight and long duration operation.

companies.name: Lymbic AI
companies.description:
Problem
AI-generated deepfakes have breached military and diplomatic systems by mimicking trusted voices and faces. Static biometrics—face, voice, fingerprint—can be cloned or leaked. 

Solution
Lymbic’s Neuroprint uses brainwave signals (EEG) to verify identity in real time. By triggering neural responses (e.g., P300, SSVEP), we authenticate users with non-observable, live-only signals—impossible to spoof or replay.

Field Validation
Pre-field. Lab-tested on consumer EEG devices (e.g., Muse 2, BrainBit), achieving 84.2% accuracy and 93.5% specificity. Letters of intent from BrainBit, UltraPCS, and Idun Technologies.

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 4. Validated neural challenge-response authentication protocol using evoked EEG signals. 

Strategic Advantage
Evoked-signal protocol + cryptographic unlock pipeline. IP pending. Access to proprietary brainprint datasets. Competitors lack zero-knowledge EEG frameworks or live-signal detection capability.

Go-to-Market Access
Engaging SBIR, AFWERX, and DIU. Registered with SAM.gov. Early contact with SOCOM, JSOC, and defense primes through OODAloop, Black Hat, and SCSP networks.

Dual-Use Potential
Neuroprint extends to secure wearables (AR/VR headsets, earbuds), AI safety (identity-linked model access), and continuous identity verification in fintech and healthcare.

Team
Nathaniel Rose, ex-Circle ML engineer, Imperial neurotech alum. Advised by ex-DARPA PMs and cybersecurity leads from Microsoft and Harvard.

Competitive Landscape
Traditional biometrics are cloneable. We’re the first to fuse evoked EEG with cryptographic authentication.

Primary User
SOCOM operators, UAV pilots, and cyber personnel accessing classified systems.

User-Critical Problem
Current MFA fails under duress. Users can’t prove they’re present or human. Neuroprint verifies identity continuously—even mid-mission.

companies.name: Decisive
companies.description:
Small Special Operations Forces teams have access to some of the most advanced reconnaissance tools in the world but team leaders are rarely able to see what the sensors see in real time! This disconnect creates a mission-critical problem: SOF operators make split-second tactical decisions based on outdated intelligence while their ISR platforms collect real-time data that never reaches them.
Our product, the Distributed Data Framework, is an open source data integration and interoperability platform that enables federated data discovery, processing, transformation, and dissemination. We've deployed operationally across NGA enterprise GEOINT discovery systems, Air Force intelligence processing applications, C2 messaging for high altitude platforms, and SOF team integration at classified exercises.
At TRL 9, our system is fully operational and proven in mission-critical environments across multiple DoD programs with demonstrated reliability and interoperability. Founder Jay McNallie brings deep intelligence system architectural experience from Octo/Connexta, combined with an open-source approach that prevents vendor lock-in while enabling NATO interoperability capabilities.
Market access comes through established DoD relationships and direct contracts with NGA and USAF. Beyond defense, our federated data mesh extends to energy grid monitoring, supply chain logistics, and border security.
While competitors like Palantir offer proprietary solutions, we differentiate through open standards delivering superior real-time data access from enterprise to tactical edge. For SOF team leaders commanding world-class sensors but unable to access real-time feeds, our framework transforms reconnaissance capabilities into immediate tactical advantage when seconds matter most.

companies.name: Nighthawk Integrated Solutions LLC
companies.description:
Problem
OverWatch Locator (OWL) was initially designed for firefighters operating in the austere fire conditions inside structures such as high thermal temperatures, zero visibility from smoke, structural collapse, etc. 

Solution
OWL is a deployable 3D interior tracking system designed around ultra wideband (UWB) to dramatically reduce the number of deaths/severe injuries across the community by reducing the exposure of firefighters through decreasing search & rescue time from minutes to seconds.

Field Validation
Field tested multiple times. Full scale tech demonstrated October 2023.

Tech Maturity (TRL)
TRL 6

Strategic Advantage
The tracking tech that powers OWL is a first of its kind deployable 3D tracking system. Patented in: USA (3 issued, 1 pending), Canada (1 issued), European Union (1 pending).

Go-to-Market Access
AFWERX, AFRL, DHS S&T.

Dual-Use Potential
From the beginning, OWL has been designed to meet or exceed MIL SPEC to allow for use in DoD with no changes to the tech.

Team
Phil Taber is a 22-year USAF combat veteran, USAF Weapons School Graduate (Top Gun), squadron commander, operational test evaluator and was inducted into the USAF Safety Hall of Fame. 

Competitive Landscape
The primary competitor in the market space is Ascent Integrated Tech and uses an iPhone 16 "Find My Phone" style app for tracking. Does not work in high thermal temperatures environments.

Primary User
First Responders (firefighters, law enforcement), Small Teams (SOF, FBI HRT, SWAT, JTAC, etc.), Ships (interior/exterior), Underground Facilities (Blue Personnel Tracking), Subterrain Ops (UGF and tunnel complexes), hospitals, etc.

User-Critical Problem
Interior tracking is the Holy Grail is firefighting tech and is desperately needed save lives and reduce severe injuries.

companies.name: Lionfish Cyber Security
companies.productName: Cyber Tackle Box™ Workforce & Compliance Platform
companies.description:
Problem
The DoD faces a shortfall of 28,000+ cyber professionals — a direct threat to U.S. military readiness. Critical missions, networks, and weapon systems are exposed. Without trained defenders, adversaries can breach systems faster than we can fill vacancies. This is no longer a hiring issue — it's a national defense emergency.

Solution
Lionfish trains college students in CMMC/CCP, deploys them to help DoD contractors meet compliance, then transitions them into DoD cyber roles — closing the talent gap and securing the supply chain.

Field Validation
Pilot at Ball State and Production currently at Northern Virginia Community College. Students completed CCP certification and will support live CMMC compliance projects for DoD contractors.

Technology Maturity
TRL 7–8: Platform and model actively used. Students perform real documentation, control implementation, and walk-throughs.

Strategic Advantage
Patent-pending AI training, GRC platform, community talent pipeline, and “By, With, and Through” deployment — unmatched in scope and integration.

Go-to-Market Access
University pipelines, primes, DIU, AFCEA, and outreach to USINDOPACOM J6/J8 and DoD cyber workforce leads.

Dual-Use Potential
Expandable to energy, healthcare, education, and critical infrastructure.

Team
Jeremy Miller (Green Beret, IBJ Tech Exec), Dr. Diane Janosek (former NSA exec), Dr. Shadi Jawhar (CTO).

Competitive Edge
Others offer tools. Lionfish offers both tools and builds the people who defend the nation.

Primary Users
USINDOPACOM J6/J8, cyber workforce PMs, supply chain officers, and student Cyber Sentinels™.

Without trained defenders, adversaries can breach systems faster than we can fill vacancies. This is no longer a hiring issue — it's a national defense emergency.

companies.name: Ardent MC
companies.description:
Problem
Only 2% of the Intelligence Community's data is assessed by analysts. There's too much data and there will never be enough analysts to comb through it, leaving threats unseen and unaddressed. 

Solution
A multi-agent system processes all the data, builds a knowledge graph to connect the dots, and allows users to query for information in plain language. 

Field Validation
Pre-field

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL 3. We have built a proof of concept of the solution. 

Strategic Advantage
We are leveraging our 20 years of experience as a government contractor to speed through the standard barriers to entry and leverage an open-source tech stack to quickly add value to users. 

Go-to-Market Access
We are pursuing SBIRs and BAAs to build and get user feedback, then leverage that towards a direct contract. 

Dual-Use Potential
Any industry that needs to know where and when. Logistics for monitoring and assessing supply lines, energy for grid monitoring and maintenance, agriculture for crop management, etc.

Team
Richard Zareck II, CEO, 25 years tech entrepreneurship
Mireille Estephan, CTO, ex-AWS, ex-Capital One tech leader
Trevor Moore, director of technology, 10 years of R&D for defense

Competitive Landscape
Our biggest competition is Palantir with Project Maven. We are going beyond computer vision to aggregate information and insight from detections and classifications created by programs like them. 

Primary User
Geospatial intel analyst.

User-Critical Problem
Analysts must provide information to decision-makers in a timely fashion. But with too much data to assess, they focus on tracking problems they already know about, instead of looking for new ones or at the big picture.

companies.name: Agventure Tech INC
companies.description:
Problem
Brigade Support Battalions rely on warfighters to provide transport, security, and logistics support, creating operational strain across MOS roles.
Solution
AUV-1 delivers remote-controlled and autonomous capabilities to transport casualties to extraction points, carry supplies to forward operations, and establish perimeter defense. 
Field Validation
Pre-field. We have completed the design and build phases of our prototype. 
Technology Maturity (TRL)
We are currently at TRL 5.
Strategic Advantage
AUV-1 GVW: 2,500 lbs Power: 20 kWh Payload: 1,000 lb payload Range: 8 hours / 200 miles. IP: integrates 80% off-the-shelf, fully CAN-enabled components into a cloud-connected, agile, multipurpose, price point: 250k
Go-to-Market Access
Our primary commercial focus has been agriculture. 
Dual-Use Potential
AUV-1 supports med evac, perimeter defense, supply transport.
Team
Carl D. Hays III – CEO/CPO: 15 years in precision navigation, IoT systems, and product R&D; doctoral candidate
Kevin Bentley – Principal Engineer: Navigation and autonomy; 
Eric Del Real – Principal Electrical Engineer: MS in system controls; 
Cody Bateman – Principal Mechanical Engineer: System design and COTS integration expert
Competitive Landscape
Current AUVs are often overly specialized, underpowered, or oversized. AUV-1 fills a critical gap in the Light Duty AUV space with balance, flexibility, and affordability.
Primary User
68W, 68J, 92Y, 88N 
User-Critical Problem
Warfighters are forced to split focus between mission-critical roles and logistics. AUV-1 restores focus to core MOS functions—saving lives, resupplying units, and protecting perimeters—enhancing force multiplication with fewer assets.

companies.name: Sheridan Technologies
companies.productName: MAIO (Modular AI Overwatch) a Modular AI Autonomy Platform for ISR and intercept
companies.technologyArea: Trusted AI / Autonomy,Integrated Sensing and Cyber,Aerospace
companies.description:
Sheridan Technologies is an agile engineering firm with a proven DoD track record, making systems smarter, from software to hardware.

Problem
Warfighters in GPS-denied, RF-contested areas lack autonomous ISR and counter-UAS capabilities without satellites or remote pilots.

Solution
MAIO (Modular AI Overwatch) is an edge-AI sensor-fusion module that detects, tracks, and intercepts threats without GPS or comms. It guides ISR drones, powers munitions, and upgrades legacy systems. Fusing EO/IR, LiDAR, acoustic, passive RF, MAIO eliminates false positives for fire-and-forget targeting with zero RF emissions. 

Field Validation
360° tracking and live-fire intercepts on quadrotors and 70 mm missiles in GPS-denied environments. Tests show 85% UAS kill rates.

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL-7: Prototype proven in GPS-denied, live-fire, sensor-fusion tests.

Strategic Advantage
Consensus AI (no false positives); edge-optimized (<30 W, no radios/satellites); modular open standards; unique EO/IR, LiDAR, RF & acoustic fusion.

Go-to-Market Access
Self-funded via DoD contracts; in SBIR/xTech; using demos for R\&D; embedding in UAV/C-UAS; building loitering ISR UAV.

Dual-Use Potential
Perimeter defense & surveillance; SWAT/military clearing: autonomous room mapping & threat location.

Team
James Sheridan, CEO & architect, 10+ years in avionics (F-35, guided missiles);
Robert Briody, EE, 30+ years in Aerospace & Defense (Collins, Raytheon, Ball Aerospace).

Competitive Landscape
Startups like Anduril rely on GPS/RF emitters; primes (L3Harris, Raytheon) use bulky, satellite-dependent kits.

Primary User
Tactical ISR teams, C-UAS operators, and small-unit leaders needing rapid detection & response.

User-Critical Problem
Operators juggle sensor feeds, jammed GPS, and remote pilots, causing delays and missed targets. MAIO removes latency and lets them focus on mission decisions.

companies.name: Quantum Research Sciences
companies.description:
Problem
Any warfighter making decisions within a large combinatorial situation...and most military decisions involve large combinatorial situations...is either lucky or wrong.  This is because no classical tool exists for delivering complex multi-variate solutions in real-time.

Solution
Our desktop-based, quantum optimization interface replaces spreadsheet (or even whiteboard) heuristics, estimations, approximations and guesses.

Field Validation
Our technology is Phase III and in production operation with AFMC.

Technology Maturity (TRL)
TRL9 (in use for AFMC at Tinker AFB).

Strategic Advantage
Patented

Go-to-Market Access
5 SBIR/STTR

Dual-Use Potential
We have commercial interest. We have 1 prime partner.  

Team
We have 2 of the nation's top physicists, 1 active-duty AF physicist (TDY) and several Purdue University top Computer Scientist programmers.

Competitive Landscape
There are 3 other US-based SBC's that are delivering quantum software.   However, we are the leader in the DoD space and the only company to have quantum software in (non-R&D) production.

Primary User
Inventory managers and battle managers

User-Critical Problem
Anyone that needs to prioritize multiple choices across multiple constrains can leverage our technology.

companies.name: Starcycle
companies.productName: Defense OS: Agentic AI Workflow Engine
companies.description:
Problem
Regulated, multiparty workflows (procurement, IP diligence) create cognitive load, rework, and opaque handoffs. Policy shifts outrun templates; rationale buried in email. Cycles stretch; fielding slips.

Solution
Starcycle is an AI-first, human-in-the-loop platform that turns policy into in-flow guardrails using a policy-aware KG and OODA loops—delivering first-pass packages, explainable decisions, and automated handoffs. Modular extension: Defense OS.

Field Validation
Production deployment in fintech on the same engine. Defense: operational prototypes for procurement and IP/patent diligence; pre-production on-prem/air-gapped builds available.

Technology Maturity (TRL)
Core engine: production-proven (commercial). Defense OS: TRL 5–6. Next: limited deployment with a program office in a relevant DoD environment (toward TRL-7).

Strategic Advantage
Explainable-by-design with chain-of-custody; composable OODA atoms; versioned FAR/DFARS/ITAR/EAR; IBM & Red Hat deployments (gov clouds, on-prem, HPC); playbooks from fintech.

Go-to-Market Access
AFWERX/SBIR/DIU; primes; direct program offices; SigmaBlox.

Dual-Use Potential
CPA SaaS (Q3 2025); public-sector procurement; private equity; utilities/critical infra; healthcare; enterprise GRC.

Team
Luke Schantz (CTO), Jaclyn Sui (CEO), and founding engineers in HITL/KG automation; IBM & Red Hat ecosystem.

Competitive Landscape
GovCon suites, RPA, and data platforms solve slices. Starcycle unifies KG + OODA + HITL for first-pass, auditable packages; deployable on-prem/air-gapped.

Primary User
Contracting specialists/KOs, PMO leads, capture managers; legal/compliance analysts.

User-Critical Problem
Need real-time validation, remediation, and logged custody to hit first-pass approvals and predictable timelines.

companies.name: Intelligenic
companies.description:
Problem
The Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) is highly manual, resulting in inefficiencies, high costs, and long delivery times. This inefficiency leads to $2.6T in expenses tied to unfulfilled stakeholder needs, quality issues, and incomplete software (SW) projects.
Solution
Our platform automates the SDLC, covering discovery, design, code generation, quality assurance, and deployment by dynamically generating outcomes that typically require manual effort from SDLC pros.
Field Validation
Our product is launching on 8/4/25, targeting nearly 1,000 SDLC professionals on our waiting list across many industries.
TRL
TRL is ~3. We are set to begin our first Phase I SBIR with the DoD CDAO to support their Gen AI initiatives to enhance the SW development (dev) processes.
Strategic Advantage
•	Unique SDLC data allowing users to produce superior SDLC content
•	Integration of SDLC tools producing dynamically generated content in them
•	Automation of all SDLC activities.
GTM
We will soon commence our first Phase I SBIR with the DoD CDAO, marking our entry into defense and informing our future strategies.
Dual-Use Potential
Our primary focus is on commercial sector technology firms engaged in custom SW dev.
Team
•	Noel Wilson, CEO & Founder: 28 years in SW dev and GTM
•	Ryan Riley, Strategic Advisor for Defense: Retired Air Force Lt. Colonel
•	The Team: 100+ years in SW dev
Competitive Landscape
Inteligenic operates in a competitive SDLC automation market, including large and small players such as OpenAI, Microsoft, and Cursor.
Primary User
SW engineers working in units such as the Army Software Factory and Air Force Software Engineering Groups.
User Critical Problem
Users face excessive time and effort in SW creation, often lacking the necessary expertise to complete tasks effectively.

companies.name: EvoLux Transportation, LLC
companies.productName: EvoLux's Air Charter Marketplace and SkyShare Reservation Platform
companies.description:
Problem
DoD personnel rely on outdated systems like DTS and Space-A Flights under TRANSCOM’s Air Mobility Command. Modernization is essential to improve air logistics.
Solution 
EvoLux offers an air charter marketplace and reservation system that distributes VTOL and fixed-wing aircraft availability in real & future time with an intuitive UI. We propose integrating into DTS and customizing our SaaS platform to enhance Space-A flight management. Additionally, we can offer our existing commercial platform through MWR programs, giving service members & families access to discounted flights
Field Validation
Now on version 7, EvoLux has iterated since 2014. We launched SkyShare reservations with Rolls-Royce at five track events (2015–2018), scaling to 18 flights/day. We've since expanded to include fixed-wing operators & distribute empty legs. We're cash flow positive & preparing for Series A financing to scale B2C and launch our B2B SaaS
Technology Maturity
Our platform is globally deployed, but currently only marketed in the U.S.
Strategic Advantage
We've built the most comprehensive vtol & fixed-wing marketplace. While others replicate features, our platform remains unmatched
Go-to-Market
We seek to meet w/ the Small Business Programs Office, AFWERX, and TRANSCOM's Defense Innovation Unit to demo, align with an RFP, or explore a direct contract
Dual-Use Potential
DoD fleets benefit from the SaaS platform for logistics, while personnel use the commercial app off-duty
Team
Founder/CEO Raymond Leavitt (Sikorsky winner), CIO Matt Lally, Josh Drabik (Marketing). Advisory Board: Scott Ashton, Shaan Bhanji, Dragos Dumitrascu
Competitors
Blade, VistaJet, XOJet, JetSuiteX, WheelsUp. EvoLux is the only FAA-compliant social-reservation experience for all mission-specific aircraft

companies.name: Optimized Optics, Inc.
companies.technologyArea: Advanced Computing and Software,Trusted AI / Autonomy
companies.description:
Optics support defense systems from laser communications to Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance sensors, and commercial systems such as virtual reality glasses and self-driving vehicles. With laser communications, forces can communicate with greater bandwidth and smaller risk. And sensors are the limiting factor in problems from autonomy to counter-UAV systems. Optical materials have advanced rapidly, but humans' ability to use them has not kept up.

Artificial intelligence can explore more designs, helping humans find the best fit faster. With AI, we can design for real world conditions at the beginning rather than identify issues late in the process.

Our strategic advantage is that we understand both the problem and the solution. Aaron Margolis is an AI expert featured in the New York Times, and Alan Geller is an optical systems designer with almost 20 years of experience supporting both commercial and defense customers and 12 years running his own firm.

Our biggest competitive concern is existing optical systems design software firms, who are too committed to current products to disrupt the market. Another startup working on AI for opitc but focused on a 3D printed lens with limited capability.

We are pre-field, still at level 2 TRL, but planning for validation in 2026. We're doing problem discovery and looking for partners in research labs, original equipment manufacturers, and primes. We're applying to SBIRs, competitions, and other RFPs. Example problems include helping naval vessels and planes communicate without giving away positions. Another is autonomous ISR units sending data without fiber optic cables.

Every year, optics supports $2T of commercial products, from cameras to diagnostics, and $400B in defense. SATCOM is a $150B field, ripe for disruption from lasers with 5x the bandwidth.  And all these areas are all growing at 5-10% annually.

companies.name: XR 2 LEAD LLC
companies.productName: Mariner-SIM (Maritine Adv RF Training and readiness Simulator)
companies.description:
MARINER-SIM (Maritime Advanced RF Training Simulator)
Problem: SOF and naval operators face mission-critical communication failures during maritime ops due to atmospheric ducting and sea-state interference that current training doesn't address.

Solution: MARINER-SIM simulates real-time maritime RF propagation effects, teaching operators to recognize and adapt to ocean-specific communication challenges through immersive training.
Status: TRL 3 - Feasibility complete, ready to prototype. Pre-field validation phase.
Strategic Advantage: First to adapt validated AREPS algorithms for operator training; requires deep RF engineering and maritime environmental modeling expertise.
Market: Pursuing SOCOM/Navy contracts, SBIRs, BAAs. Dual-use: offshore energy/shipping operations face identical RF challenges.

Team:
Marryam Chaudhry, PI - XR2LEAD CEO, 20+ years AI-driven defense simulation/VR training. NATO-DIANA mentor, led SOCOM SBIR projects, 2023 STTR AEROD with Georgia Tech GTRI.
Fred McWilliams - MIT Lincoln Labs, 20+ years maritime training simulators/RF propagation systems.
Ron Dahlgren - Senior software engineer, M&S/AI expert, former 82nd Airborne with RF ops experience.

companies.synopsisRaw:
MARINER-SIM (Maritime Advanced RF Training Simulator)
Problem: SOF and naval operators face mission-critical communication failures during maritime ops due to atmospheric ducting and sea-state interference that current training doesn't address.

Solution: MARINER-SIM simulates real-time maritime RF propagation effects, teaching operators to recognize and adapt to ocean-specific communication challenges through immersive training.
Status: TRL 3 - Feasibility complete, ready to prototype. Pre-field validation phase.
Strategic Advantage: First to adapt validated AREPS algorithms for operator training; requires deep RF engineering and maritime environmental modeling expertise.
Market: Pursuing SOCOM/Navy contracts, SBIRs, BAAs. Dual-use: offshore energy/shipping operations face identical RF challenges.
